symptom: Application Insights not capturing HTTP 404 request telemetry - customer claims 404s visible in IIS but missing from App Insights requests table
rootCause: The HTTP 404 status code was set by IIS Web Core module (MODULE_SET_RESPONSE_ERROR_STATUS with ModuleName=IIS Web Core), not by the ASP.NET ManagedPipelineHandler. Application Insights SDK only captures telemetry at the .NET managed layer and is unaware of webserver-level events.
solution: Check FREB logs for MODULE_SET_RESPONSE_ERROR_STATUS entry. If ModuleName is IIS Web Core (not ManagedPipelineHandler), the 404 is set at IIS layer before reaching managed code. Customer needs to modify IIS config to route the request through the managed handler so App Insights can capture it. Same behavior applies to both codeless and SDK instrumentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Diagnostics and Tools/Tools/Capture Failed Request Event Buffering logs (FREB Logs)

symptom: FREB (Failed Request Event Buffering) logs are truncated - XML output file is incomplete due to large file size
rootCause: Default maxLogFileSizeKB for FREB logs is too small, causing truncation when request processing generates large trace output.
solution: Create applicationHost.xdt file at d:\home\site\applicationHost.xdt with XDT transform to increase traceFailedRequestsLogging settings: set maxLogFileSizeKB to 4096/8000/16000 and maxLogFiles to 100 (default is 50). Restart the app after applying. Verify transform took effect at D:\local\Config\applicationhost.config.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Diagnostics and Tools/Tools/Capture Failed Request Event Buffering logs (FREB Logs)

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Diagnostics and Tools/Tools/Capture Failed Request Event Buffering logs (FREB Logs)

symptom: Application startup time increases after adding Application Insights .NET/.NET Core SDK due to byte-code instrumentation
rootCause: Byte-code instrumentation setup disables certain .NET Runtime startup optimizations and adds recompilation of instrumented methods
solution: 1) Discontinue use of profiling libraries to collect SQL command name. 2) Warm the application instance up before adding to the rotation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Existing Material from PG Wiki/Troubleshooting performance issues when Application Insights .Net or .Net core SDK library is a suspect

symptom: RPS drop or CPU increase immediately after application startup under high RPS with Application Insights SDK
rootCause: Adaptive Sampling's first evaluation during steep RPS incline may be inaccurate, allowing higher sampling rate for ~2 minutes causing computational and memory overhead
solution: Configure AdaptiveSamplingTelemetryProcessor with InitialSamplingPercentage set to desired initial rate, or shorten evaluation period / SamplingPercentageDecreaseTimeout.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Existing Material from PG Wiki/Troubleshooting performance issues when Application Insights .Net or .Net core SDK library is a suspect

symptom: Continuous CPU/RPS degradation throughout application lifetime at high RPS with Application Insights SDK
rootCause: Disabling adaptive sampling leads to noticeable overhead; even with sampling enabled, telemetry collection/initialization consumes CPU; effect worsens as CPU approaches 100%. Minimal sampling rate 0.1% still processes ~50 items at 10000 RPS.
solution: 1) 1-10% overhead is expected range. 2) Ensure adaptive sampling is enabled and rates are correct (check ItemCount in Request table). 3) Scale up/out to keep CPU below 90% without AI so with AI it stays under 100%. 4) Remove unnecessary Telemetry Initializers.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Existing Material from PG Wiki/Troubleshooting performance issues when Application Insights .Net or .Net core SDK library is a suspect

symptom: Memory consumption shows saw-like pattern with Application Insights SDK - quick rise to high memory, abrupt drop, repeat (looks like memory leak with restarts)
rootCause: Server garbage collection (GC) works differently from desktop GC - it executes cleanup less frequently after memory reaches ~35% of available memory threshold. This is normal server GC behavior on multi-processor systems.
solution: 1) No action needed - this is how server GC was designed to operate. 2) Optionally replace server GC with desktop GC: set <gcServer enabled="false"/> in <runtime> configuration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Existing Material from PG Wiki/Troubleshooting performance issues when Application Insights .Net or .Net core SDK library is a suspect

symptom: Memory consumption linearly increases and never goes down with Application Insights SDK (true memory leak pattern)
rootCause: Manual telemetry submission code creates multiple long-lifetime AI objects. Common pattern: instantiating TelemetryConfiguration or TelemetryChannel on every request or every Azure Function invocation, creating orphaned threads.
solution: Review usage of telemetry classes from Application Insights libraries. Find patterns that instantiate many long-lifetime objects like TelemetryConfiguration. Refactor to use a few shared long-lifetime objects.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Existing Material from PG Wiki/Troubleshooting performance issues when Application Insights .Net or .Net core SDK library is a suspect

symptom: TCP connection exhaustion or timeouts related to Application Insights SDK
rootCause: Multiple TelemetryConfiguration objects each create their own channel, leading to connection competition. Older SDK versions (<2.7.1) had HTTP Client connection leak. AI endpoint/Live Metrics timeouts can cause connection buildup.
solution: 1) Update to Application Insights SDK v2.7.1+ (earlier versions had HTTP Client connection leak). 2) Review and refactor code to reduce TelemetryConfiguration/TelemetryChannel instances - ideally single instance.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Existing Material from PG Wiki/Troubleshooting performance issues when Application Insights .Net or .Net core SDK library is a suspect

symptom: Response time increase and lock contention in System.Diagnostics.Trace logs after updating Application Insights Trace Listener SDK to 2.3+
rootCause: autoflush="true" setting in web.config causes Flush() to be called after each log message. SDK 2.3+ implements Flush() which creates lock contention in telemetry items buffer access.
solution: 1) Do not use autoflush="true" in production. 2) Use Application Insights Trace Listener 2.2 which does not implement Flush(). Tip: Filter PerfView traces on 'TelemetlyClientFlush' to confirm autoflush issue by checking flush call frequency.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Existing Material from PG Wiki/Troubleshooting performance issues when Application Insights .Net or .Net core SDK library is a suspect

symptom: File system access contention and race conditions between multiple Application Insights channels
rootCause: Multiple identical ServerTelemetryChannel instances are configured (intentionally or by mistake), competing for the same local folder for retries and leveraging the same internal components, creating race conditions.
solution: Review usage of telemetry classes from Application Insights libraries. Find patterns that instantiate multiple TelemetryConfiguration/TelemetryChannel objects. Refactor to use preferably only one instance.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Existing Material from PG Wiki/Troubleshooting performance issues when Application Insights .Net or .Net core SDK library is a suspect

symptom: Application Insights SDK performance issue - generic investigation needed (CPU/RPS/Memory/Lock contention)
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-d-ai-sdk-performance-investigation-flow.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Existing Material from PG Wiki/Troubleshooting performance issues when Application Insights .Net or .Net core SDK library is a suspect

symptom: Attempts to create or update tags on Application Insights resources fail with 500 InternalServerError containing AADSTS7000112: Application Insights Configuration Service (AppId 6a0a243c-0886-468a-a4c2-eff52c7445da) is disabled
rootCause: The Application Insights Configuration Service first-party app has been disabled in Microsoft Entra ID, blocking authentication flows required for control plane tag operations on AI resources
solution: Re-enable the Application Insights Configuration Service enterprise application in Microsoft Entra ID. Guidance: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/disable-user-sign-in-portal
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/106818

symptom: Diagnostic settings are not deleted when the associated Azure resource is deleted or migrated, resulting in orphaned diagnostic settings that are no longer accessible but still exist
rootCause: Diagnostic settings are extension proxy resources of the Azure resource they are associated with. The delete action on the parent resource does not cascade to related diagnostic settings, leaving them orphaned and inaccessible
solution: Option 1: Recreate the Azure resource under the same subscription, resource group, and resource name to make the diagnostic settings accessible again, then delete them before re-deleting the resource. Option 2: Submit a PG escalation request via https://aka.ms/azmonpgescalation to remove the orphaned settings. For automated processes, include a step to delete diagnostic settings BEFORE deleting the associated Azure resource.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/14416

symptom: Need to find Application Insights component details (name, subscription, resource group) when only an instrumentation key (iKey) or component name is available
solution:
Use Kusto query against the AI backend cluster:

```kql
cluster('aibeftprd.kusto.windows.net').database('Cdsproddatabase').ActiveComponents()
| where iKey == "<ikey guid here>"
```

Or search by component name:
```kql
cluster('aibeftprd.kusto.windows.net').database('Cdsproddatabase').ActiveComponents()
| where name =~ "<component name here>"
```
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Kusto/Query for Application Insights Component

symptom: Tagging operations (create/update) on Application Insights resources fail with 500 InternalServerError: AADSTS7000112 Application Insights Configuration Service (6a0a243c-0886-468a-a4c2-eff52c7445da) is disabled
rootCause: The Application Insights Configuration Service first-party app (App ID: 6a0a243c-0886-468a-a4c2-eff52c7445da) has been disabled in Microsoft Entra ID, blocking authentication flows required for tag control plane operations on Application Insights resources.
solution: Re-enable the Application Insights Configuration Service in Microsoft Entra ID. Guidance: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/disable-user-sign-in-portal?pivots=portal
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/106818

symptom: Diagnostic settings become orphaned (inaccessible but still existing) after the associated Azure resource is deleted or migrated across Resource Groups/Subscriptions
rootCause: Diagnostic settings are extension proxy resources of the Azure resource. The delete action against the Azure resource does not cascade to the related diagnostic settings, leaving them orphaned. This is by-design behavior.
solution: Option 1: Recreate the Azure resource under the same subscription, resource group, and resource name to make the orphaned diagnostic settings accessible again, then delete them before re-deleting the resource. Option 2: Submit a PG escalation request via https://aka.ms/azmonpgescalation to have orphaned diagnostic settings removed. For automated processes, include a step to delete diagnostic settings BEFORE deleting the associated Azure resource.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/14416

symptom: Application Insights auto-instrumentation conflicts with existing SDK instrumentation on App Service Linux Web App; duplicate or missing telemetry
rootCause: AppAlreadyInstrumented flag is true in status JSON file (/home/LogFiles/ApplicationInsights/status/status_*.json), indicating the application already has SDK instrumentation that conflicts with codeless auto-instrumentation.
solution: 1) Navigate to KUDU Advanced Tools > BASH. 2) Check status file: cd LogFiles/ApplicationInsights/status && cat status_*.json. 3) If AppAlreadyInstrumented=true: Option A - disable auto-instrumentation in App Service blade (keep SDK); Option B - enable PreemptSdk to stop SDK custom telemetry and yield to auto-instrumentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Diagnostics and Tools/Status Page - App Services Web Apps and Application Insights/Net (Core) Running on Linux Web App

symptom: FREB (Failed Request Event Buffering) logs are truncated; incomplete request trace data on App Service
rootCause: Default FREB log file size and maxLogFiles (50) are too small for complex requests. When maxLogFiles limit reached, half the files are deleted.
solution: Create applicationHost.xdt in d:\home\site\ with increased maxLogFileSizeKB (4096/8000/16000) and maxLogFiles (100). Restart app after changes. Verify transform at D:\local\Config\applicationhost.config. Reference: https://github.com/projectkudu/kudu/wiki/Xdt-transform-samples
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Diagnostics and Tools/Tools/Capture Failed Request Event Buffering logs (FREB Logs)

symptom: App Services Auto Instrumentation (.NET) fails with 'Could not load file or assembly System.Runtime.CompilerServices.Unsafe.dll' after ANT build version update (98.0.7.575 → 98.0.10.735). App Insights extension 2.8.44 causes apps to fail to initialize or startup.
rootCause: New App Insights extension 2.8.44 pulls a newer version of System.Runtime.CompilerServices.Unsafe.dll (4.0.4.1), which conflicts with dormant bindingRedirect entries in the customer's web.config (pointing to version 4.0.6.0). If the dll is not present in the app's \bin folder, the redirect causes a FileNotFoundException.
solution: Option 1: If System.Runtime.CompilerServices.Unsafe.dll is NOT in the \bin folder, remove the <bindingRedirect> for that assembly from web.config, or change it to point to version 4.0.4.1. Option 2: If the dll IS in \bin folder, temporarily set ApplicationInsightsAgent_EXTENSION_VERSION=2.8.37 in App Service settings to revert to the older extension. Use AppLens 'Antares Version' and 'Application Insights Auto Instrumentation' detectors to check versions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/34102

symptom: The 'client_OS' field in 'browserTimings' and 'pageViews' tables in Application Insights reports incorrect or inaccurate OS data. For example, Windows 11 clients are reported as 'Windows 10'.
rootCause: Since Windows 11, browsers have frozen the User-Agent header value, making it no longer a reliable source for populating client_OS in Application Insights telemetry.
solution: Currently no fix available. Microsoft plans to investigate viable workarounds using User-Agent Client Hints. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-edge/web-platform/how-to-detect-win11
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/37425

symptom: Java application with Application Insights Java Agent experiences high CPU usage caused by Reactor and Reactor Netty instrumentations
rootCause: The Reactor and Reactor Netty instrumentations applied by the Java Agent can significantly increase CPU usage. Can verify by enabling self-diagnostics at DEBUG level and checking for 'Applying instrumentation: reactor' and 'Applying instrumentation: reactor-netty' in logs.
solution: Disable Reactor/Reactor Netty instrumentations by setting environment variables OTEL_INSTRUMENTATION_REACTOR_ENABLED=false and OTEL_INSTRUMENTATION_REACTOR_NETTY_ENABLED=false, or use JVM system properties -Dotel.instrumentation.reactor.enabled=false and -Dotel.instrumentation.reactor.netty.enabled=false
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Troubleshooting performance impact involving Java agent

symptom: Application Insights Java Agent causes performance issues including high CPU, high memory, slow startup, application crashes or general slowness
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-troubleshooting-performance-java-agent.md

symptom: Starting, stopping, or creating an Availability Test in Application Insights can take several minutes to take effect. Stopping a test may still result in one or more additional test runs before the status change is processed.
rootCause: By Design. There is a queueing mechanism to notify the backend instances running the test. Multiple factors affect how fast this queue is processed, causing fluctuating delays.
solution: No workaround. This is by-design behavior. Set customer expectation that status changes may take several minutes to propagate to all test regions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/40384

symptom: No SQL Dependency telemetry in Application Insights for .NET Framework 4.x application using Microsoft.Data.SqlClient with Profiler enabled. Other telemetry types (Requests, Traces, some Dependencies) are present but SQL calls are missing.
rootCause: Application Insights SDK only byte-code instruments System.Data.SqlClient classes via Profiler. When Profiler is enabled, Microsoft.Data.SqlClient calls are not hooked, so SQL Dependency data is not collected. GitHub issue: microsoft/ApplicationInsights-dotnet#2736.
solution: Option 1: Disable the Profiler — SDK falls back to EventSource which supports Microsoft.Data.SqlClient. For App Service: stop ApplicationInsightsProfiler3 webjob. For Azure VMs: disable InstrumentationEngine via Application Insights Agent. Option 2: Switch application code from Microsoft.Data.SqlClient to System.Data.SqlClient to keep Profiler and still capture SQL Dependency telemetry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/40472

symptom: Application Insights auto-instrumentation (App Services attach / VM extension / on-premises agent) stops reporting telemetry after code deployment or when first enabled. Conflicting DLLs detected in wwwroot/bin: Microsoft.ApplicationInsights, System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource, or Microsoft.AspNet.TelemetryCorrelation.
rootCause: Auto-instrumentation variants depend on specific versions of certain NuGet packages. If the application introduces a different version of these DLLs, auto-instrumentation detects the conflict and backs off, leaving the app uninstrumented.
solution: Remove the conflicting package to let auto-instrumentation attach. If removal is not feasible (e.g. System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource is a transitive dependency), switch to manual SDK instrumentation. For .NET Core on App Services, enable 'Interop with Application Insights SDK' to force auto-instrumentation despite conflicting DLLs. Diagnostics: check Kudu /ApplicationInsights endpoint, inspect wwwroot/bin for AI DLLs, or use the AppLens Auto Instrumentation detector.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/65322

symptom: Application Insights auto-instrumentation intermittently stops collecting telemetry after app restart (no code deployment). Telemetry resumes after another restart or re-enabling AI. Memory dump shows only partial AI modules loaded (missing Microsoft_AI_Web, Microsoft_AI_DependencyCollector, etc.).
rootCause: Transient DLL loading timing issue: DLLs are dynamically loaded into .NET AppDomain on demand. If a dependency like Azure.Identity.dll loads System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource.dll into the AppDomain before auto-instrumentation checks, the backoff condition triggers. On subsequent restarts, if the loading order differs, auto-instrumentation succeeds.
solution: Restart the application or disable/re-enable Application Insights to re-trigger auto-instrumentation. For persistent issues, switch to manual SDK. Verify via memory dump (lm command to check loaded modules) or App Service auto-instrumentation logs (WebAppsAppInsightsExtension table in Kusto).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/65322

symptom: Application Insights Profiler not collecting traces on Azure VM/VMSS after enabling App Insights VM Extension. Bootstrap logs show error: Standard Collector uninstallation failed with exit code 1605. Event Log shows 'Profiler bootstrap process prematurely ended with exit code 1'.
rootCause: The Profiler installer detects an existing Standard Collector Service (VSStandardCollectorService150) installed by Visual Studio or Visual Studio Remote Debugging Tools on the VM. It attempts to uninstall the conflicting service but fails with exit code 1605 ('product not currently installed' mismatch), blocking Profiler installation.
solution: Uninstall Visual Studio or Visual Studio Remote Debugging Tools from the VM. Use a clean VM without Visual Studio installed. Profiler will then install successfully and attach to .NET Framework or .NET Core apps in IIS. The Profiler team is working on a fix for a future VM extension version.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/65488

symptom: Application Insights SDK diagnostic logs not generated on App Service Web App when using SDK logging method (LogFilePath)
rootCause: LogFilePath set to C: drive path which is not accessible on App Service. C: is read-only.
solution: Set LogFilePath to D:\HOME\SDKLOGS in ApplicationInsights.config. Folder auto-created. Disable logging after collection. Collect via KUDU Debug Console.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Diagnostics and Tools/Application Insights Diagnostic Logs/dot-Net Core

symptom: Application Insights auto-instrumentation conflicts with existing SDK instrumentation on App Service Linux Web App; duplicate or missing telemetry

solution: 1) KUDU > BASH. 2) cd LogFiles/ApplicationInsights/status && cat status_*.json. 3) If AppAlreadyInstrumented=true: Option A - disable auto-instrumentation; Option B - enable PreemptSdk.

symptom: Node.js apps using Axios library produce extra failed dependency telemetry records during HTTP 301 redirects. An additional dependency record with failed status (no resultCode) appears alongside the successful 301 and final 200 response in Application Insights.
rootCause: By design — internal processing within the Axios module triggers extra HTTP events that the Application Insights Node.js SDK hooks into. The SDK team has no control over Axios internals.
solution: Three options: (1) Ignore the extra failed dependency records; (2) Replace Axios with the standard Node.js http module for that request; (3) Add a telemetry processor to drop failed dependency items: filter baseType==='RemoveDependencyData' where success===false and resultCode is empty, return false to drop.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/52155

symptom: JavaScript SDK telemetry correlation headers (W3C/HTTP) are not sent on outbound XmlHttpRequests from web pages despite enabling distributedTracingMode configuration setting.
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: (1) SDK version too old (< 2.8.12 doesn't support W3C+HTTP headers); (2) IFrames making requests without their own SDK initialization; (3) Web Workers not initializing SDK separately; (4) XMLHttpRequest polyfill overriding prototype functions before or after SDK hooks, bypassing the SDK's open/send instrumentation.
solution: Troubleshoot checklist: (1) Capture Fiddler/F12 HAR trace of outbound requests; (2) Verify SDK config follows latest docs; (3) Confirm SDK version ≥ 2.8.12 with distributedTracingMode=1; (4) Ensure IFrame pages also load JS SDK; (5) Initialize SDK in each Web Worker; (6) Check for XMLHttpRequest polyfill interference — ensure polyfill loads after AI SDK init or uses prototype references instead of cached functions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/52157

symptom: Customer receives Azure Service Health notification about deprecation of instrumentation key-based global ingestion for Application Insights and needs to identify which AI components and applications are using global ingestion endpoints
rootCause: Deprecation of instrumentation key-based global ingestion endpoints in Application Insights (EOL March 31, 2025). Customers using ikey-based global ingestion need to migrate to connection string-based regional ingestion.
solution: 1) To identify affected AI components per subscription: Use SAW device with Jarvis Actions → 'Read Telemetry Operations' → 'Check Global Ingestion' action (change endpoint from AIMON to AIPROD, paste subscription ID). Returns UseGlobal=True/False/Unknown for each component. 2) To identify instrumented applications: Run KQL query 'union AppRequests, AppDependencies, AppPageViews... | summarize count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 1d), _ResourceId, AppRoleName, AppRoleInstance, SDKVersion, Type, Url'. 3) Migrate to connection strings: https://aka.ms/MigrateToConnectionString
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/27572

symptom: Application Insights custom metrics missing from Metrics blade, metric chart gaps, alerts incorrectly firing/not firing, or Custom Metric Namespace not available in dropdown
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-e-metrics-throttling.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/27792

symptom: Custom metrics visible in customMetrics/AppMetrics log table but missing from Metrics blade charts, or metric alerts incorrectly firing/not firing despite data being ingested into Application Insights
rootCause: MDM custom metric account exceeded 50,000 active time series limit (per subscription per region, over any 12-hour span). When 'Enable alerting on custom metric dimensions' is enabled, each unique combination of metric name + dimension key + dimension value creates a separate time series, drastically expanding the count. High-cardinality dimensions (e.g., CPU percentages, session IDs) can quickly exhaust the limit.
solution: 1) Check 'Custom Metrics Opt In' setting via AIMC tool (search by ikey/name → Applications tab). 2) If set to 'WithDimension' and not needed, disable it to reduce time series count. 3) Use KQL query on customMetrics table to estimate active time series: extend json=parse_json(customDimensions), bag_unpack, distinct *, summarize TotalActiveSeries=count(). 4) Identify worst offender metrics by name with count. 5) Reduce high-cardinality dimensions. 6) Verify throttling status via Jarvis QOS dashboard: CUSTOMMETRIC_{subId}_{region}/GenevaQos.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/27792

symptom: When migrating Application Insights to workspace-based using Terraform with AzureRM version older than 3.49, the existing classic resource is deleted and recreated with the same name, resulting in loss of all historical data
rootCause: The workspace_id parameter in older AzureRM versions (<3.49) had a ForceNew condition that destroyed and recreated the Application Insights resource instead of updating it in-place
solution: Upgrade AzureRM provider to version 3.49 or greater, which fixes the workspace_id parameter to allow in-place updates without resource recreation (fix: hashicorp/terraform-provider-azurerm#21029)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/68672

symptom: After renaming or moving an Application Insights resource, ARM operations to update Availability web test metric-based alerts fail because the alert still references the old component resource URI
rootCause: Rename/move operations change the Application Insights resource URI, but the built-in metric-based alerts for Availability Tests retain a stale reference to the old component URI, causing ARM validation failures
solution: Manually update the metric-based alert ARM template to reference the new component resource URI (update the components_externalid parameter from the old name to the new name)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/69462

symptom: Application Insights SDK diagnostic logs not generated on App Service (LogFilePath on C: inaccessible)
rootCause: LogFilePath set to C: drive path which is read-only on App Service.
solution: Set LogFilePath to D:\HOME\SDKLOGS. Folder auto-created. Disable logging after collection.

symptom: Application Insights Event Counter metrics recorded as Trace telemetry with 'n/a' message and Critical severity, instead of expected customMetric. Each EventCounter metric appears as a Trace item with empty message.
rootCause: Both EventSourceTelemetryModule and EventCounterCollectionModule are enabled simultaneously. EventSource intercepts EventCounter events and writes them as Trace telemetry instead of allowing EventCounterCollectionModule to process them as customMetrics.
solution: Disable conflicting events in EventSourceTelemetryModule by adding DisabledSources for the event sources collected by EventCounterCollectionModule. Example: services.ConfigureTelemetryModule<EventSourceTelemetryModule>((module, o) => { module.DisabledSources.Add(new DisableEventSourceRequest() { Name = "Microsoft-AspNetCore-Server-Kestrel" }); }); This ensures EventCounter processes the events as metrics instead of EventSource capturing them as traces.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/61790

symptom: Application Insights Availability web tests suddenly fail in a specific region with TLS/SSL/Secure Channel errors and ~21 second duration. Errors include 'Could not establish trust relationship for the SSL/TLS secure channel' or 'Could not create SSL/TLS secure channel'.
rootCause: FedRAMP-mandated TLS 1.3 migration on Availability Test service infrastructure. Servers with TLS 1.3 enabled but improperly configured (not per RFC 8446) fail the TLS handshake. Regions progressively updated from Nov 2023 through 2024 (NCUS, SCUS, CUS, EUS, WUS2, FairFax regions).
solution: Fix TLS 1.3 configuration on the target web server per RFC 8446. Temporary mitigations: (1) Remove TLS 1.3 from server and use only TLS 1.2; (2) Change availability test to use a different, not-yet-migrated region. Diagnose by checking the TlsTransport step in test results via Executions/Results tabs. Note: servers without TLS 1.3 will negotiate down to TLS 1.2 automatically and are NOT affected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/62378

symptom: Application Insights request telemetry geo-location fields (client_City, client_StateOrProvince, client_CountryOrRegion) return blank for IPv6 client IPs
rootCause: Application Insights does not support geo-location lookup using IPv6 addresses (by design limitation)
solution: Implement a custom TelemetryInitializer to set Context.Location.* properties (City, StateOrProvince, CountryOrRegion) from the client side directly
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/84537

symptom: Permission error when creating or enabling Diagnostic Setting on Classic Application Insights component resource
rootCause: Caller lacks Contributor permissions on the Application Insights resource scope required for Diagnostic Settings creation
solution: Grant the user Contributor role on the Application Insights resource, then retry creating the Diagnostic Setting
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/87381

symptom: Heartbeat telemetry from VM is missing extra dimensions like azInst_vmId, azInst_name, azInst_location, azInst_resourceGroupName etc. Customer wants to filter heartbeat data by specific VM but these fields are absent.
rootCause: The application is not loading the AzureInstanceMetadataTelemetryModule. By default, the Application Insights SDK produces heartbeat telemetry with only basic fields (processSessionId, runtimeFramework, baseSdkTargetFramework, osType). The azInst_* fields require the AzureInstanceMetadataTelemetryModule to be explicitly enabled.
solution: Enable AzureInstanceMetadataTelemetryModule: (1) .NET Framework (ASP.NET): If using Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.Web NuGet, the module is auto-added to ApplicationInsights.config. If programmatically configuring TelemetryConfiguration, explicitly add the module. Otherwise add to ApplicationInsights.config: <Add Type="Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.WindowsServer.AzureInstanceMetadataTelemetryModule, Microsoft.AI.WindowsServer">. (2) .NET Core: See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/app/asp-net-core?tabs=netcorenew#configure-or-remove-default-telemetrymodules
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/73062

symptom: Security scanners flag Application Insights ai_user and ai_session cookies as vulnerable because they are not marked as HttpOnly
rootCause: The ai_user and ai_session cookies must be readable by the Application Insights JavaScript SDK in the browser. They cannot be set as HttpOnly because the SDK requires client-side read access to function.
solution: This is by design. Unsupported workaround documented at https://github.com/microsoft/ApplicationInsights-JS/issues/626 — customers can implement custom cookie handling, but it is not officially supported.

symptom: HTTP 500 or 500.53 errors occur after enabling Application Insights client-side monitoring via APPINSIGHTS_JAVASCRIPT_ENABLED app setting in Azure App Service
rootCause: Conflict between Application Insights automatic JavaScript snippet injection and IIS URL compression (dynamic or static). The injection modifies the response stream which is incompatible with certain compression configurations.
solution: Disable URL compression or use manual JavaScript SDK injection instead of auto-injection. See: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/azure-web-apps-net#appinsights_javascript_enabled-and-urlcompression-is-not-supported
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights setup and customization/Browser telemetry

symptom: Page visit duration (time spent on page) is not tracked in Application Insights pageViews/AppPageViews
rootCause: The autoTrackPageVisitTime configuration option is disabled by default in the Application Insights JavaScript SDK.
solution: Enable autoTrackPageVisitTime in the JavaScript SDK configuration. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/app/javascript-sdk-configuration?tabs=javascriptwebsdkloaderscript#sdk-configuration

symptom: OMS Linux agent stops collecting custom logs after file truncation or circular log rotation. No new log entries appear in the custom log table despite new data being written to the file.
rootCause: OMS agent tracks byte position in .pos file. When file is truncated/cleared to size < previously read position, agent won't collect until file grows past that position. Circular rotation (overwrite in-place) is not supported.
solution: Use standard log rotation (rename old file + create new file) instead of circular/truncate rotation. The new file gets a different inode, and the agent treats it as a new file. Check log rotation config in /etc/logrotate.d/.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Custom Logs - Linux Agent
sourceUrl: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/AzureMonitor/_wiki/wikis/AzureMonitor.wiki?pagePath=/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Custom Logs - Linux Agent

symptom: OMS Linux custom log not collected. omsagent.log shows 'WARN <PATH> does not exist. Cannot tail the file.'
rootCause: The file path specified in the custom log rule does not exist on the system. Path may be incorrect or file was removed/renamed.
solution: Verify the file path in the custom log configuration matches the actual file location on the Linux system. Check /etc/opt/microsoft/omsagent/<workspace-id>/conf/omsagent.d/customlog.conf for correct paths.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Custom Logs - Linux Agent
sourceUrl: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/AzureMonitor/_wiki/wikis/AzureMonitor.wiki?pagePath=/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Custom Logs - Linux Agent

symptom: OMS Linux custom log not collected. omsagent.log shows 'WARN <PATH> is excluded since it's unreadable or doesn't have proper permissions.'
rootCause: The omsagent process does not have read permissions on the target log file.
solution: Fix file permissions so omsagent can read the file. Check ownership and permissions with ls -la. The omsagent user (typically 'omsagent') needs read access to the log file.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Custom Logs - Linux Agent
sourceUrl: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/AzureMonitor/_wiki/wikis/AzureMonitor.wiki?pagePath=/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Custom Logs - Linux Agent

symptom: OMS Linux custom log collection stops or hangs when monitoring many files. tailfilereader process appears stuck.
rootCause: The tailfilereader hangs when monitoring >100 files or >8.5k EPS via a single custom log rule. Can also be caused by ulimit (open file limit) being exceeded.
solution: Split large wildcard rules into multiple specific rules. Avoid overly broad wildcards that match rotated files. Check ulimit with 'ulimit' command. Check open files with 'lsof | awk "{print $1}" | sort | uniq -c | sort -r -n | head'. Soft limit is 1024 files.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Custom Logs - Linux Agent
sourceUrl: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/AzureMonitor/_wiki/wikis/AzureMonitor.wiki?pagePath=/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Custom Logs - Linux Agent

symptom: OMS Linux custom log not collected. File uses non-ASCII/non-UTF-8 encoding (e.g., UTF-16).
rootCause: OMS custom log solution only supports ASCII or UTF-8 encoding. Other formats like UTF-16 are not supported.
solution: Convert log files to ASCII or UTF-8 encoding. Verify encoding with 'file full_path_to_file' command.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Custom Logs - Linux Agent
sourceUrl: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/AzureMonitor/_wiki/wikis/AzureMonitor.wiki?pagePath=/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Custom Logs - Linux Agent

symptom: MMA Windows custom logs upload delayed to Log Analytics workspace. Significant E2E ingestion latency observed.
rootCause: Application writing the custom logs is not flushing content to disk, so no file system notifications are generated for the MMA agent to detect changes.
solution: Use FileListenerTool.exe to verify file system notifications are being generated: 'FileListenerTool.exe [logs directory] [file pattern] > output.txt'. If no entries after 5-10 min, the application needs to flush logs to disk. If entries exist, collect Get-AgentInfo.ps1 logs and FileListenerTool output, then escalate via IcM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Microsoft Monitoring Agent (MMA) for Windows/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting MMA Custom Logs upload delay
sourceUrl: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/AzureMonitor/_wiki/wikis/AzureMonitor.wiki?pagePath=/Monitor Agents/Agents/Microsoft Monitoring Agent (MMA) for Windows/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting MMA Custom Logs upload delay

symptom: AMA fails to parse syslog messages. mdsd.info shows 'Invalid field APPNAME' errors. Syslog not ingested to LAW.
rootCause: Source application generates syslog messages with non-RFC3164-compliant APPNAME field containing special characters (e.g., square brackets like [RPM][8310]). AMA's mdsd parser rejects these as invalid.
solution: 1) Update the source application to generate RFC 3164 compliant syslog messages (APPNAME should be alphanumeric only). 2) Verify DCR includes the correct syslog facility - the messages may be logged under a different facility than expected (e.g., user.notice instead of daemon.info). Update DCR to include the actual facility.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/Learning Resources/Concepts/Syslog Parsing and Log Collection Issues in Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) example incident
sourceUrl: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/AzureMonitor/_wiki/wikis/AzureMonitor.wiki?pagePath=/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/Learning Resources/Concepts/Syslog Parsing and Log Collection Issues in Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) example incident

symptom: rsyslog service fails to start or shows errors after configuration change. systemctl status rsyslog shows service failed.
rootCause: Syntax error in /etc/rsyslog.conf or included config files (e.g., invalid character, escape sequence issue).
solution: Run 'journalctl -u rsyslog' to identify the exact file, line number, and character causing the error. Fix the syntax in the identified configuration file and restart rsyslog with 'systemctl restart rsyslog'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/Learning Resources/Concepts/AMA Linux: Concepts: Syslog Daemon (rsyslog)
sourceUrl: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/AzureMonitor/_wiki/wikis/AzureMonitor.wiki?pagePath=/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/Learning Resources/Concepts/AMA Linux: Concepts: Syslog Daemon (rsyslog)

symptom: syslog-ng service fails to start. systemctl status syslog-ng shows 'Failed with result exit-code' and INVALIDARGUMENT.
rootCause: Invalid syslog-ng configuration, commonly caused by: 1) Multiple internal() sources detected (e.g., custom config file duplicating system source), 2) Syntax errors in config files under /etc/syslog-ng/conf.d/.
solution: Run 'journalctl -u syslog-ng' to identify the offending configuration file and line. Either rename the problematic config to .bak extension ('mv file.conf file.conf.bak') and reload syslog-ng, or fix the configuration error directly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/Learning Resources/Concepts/AMA Linux: Concepts: Syslog Daemon (Syslog-ng)
sourceUrl: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/AzureMonitor/_wiki/wikis/AzureMonitor.wiki?pagePath=/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/Learning Resources/Concepts/AMA Linux: Concepts: Syslog Daemon (Syslog-ng)

symptom: AMA not collecting syslog on Linux. Syslog daemon config file for AMA forwarding is missing (/etc/rsyslog.d/10-AzureMonitorAgent-omfwd.conf or /etc/syslog-ng/conf.d/azuremonitoragent-tcp.conf).
rootCause: AMA only creates the forwarding config when BOTH conditions are met: 1) A DCR with valid syslog data source is associated to the VM, AND 2) The syslog daemon package (rsyslog or syslog-ng) is installed. If either condition is missing, AMA cannot create the config.
solution: Verify: 1) DCR has a valid syslog data source configured and is associated to the VM resource. 2) rsyslog or syslog-ng package is installed on the VM. Once both conditions are met, restart AMA ('systemctl restart azuremonitoragent') and verify the config file is created.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/AMA Linux/Troubleshooting Guides/AMA Linux: TSG:  Collection - Syslog
sourceUrl: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/AzureMonitor/_wiki/wikis/AzureMonitor.wiki?pagePath=/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/AMA Linux/Troubleshooting Guides/AMA Linux: TSG:  Collection - Syslog

symptom: AMA Windows not collecting IIS logs. MAEventTable shows 'Could not open directory c:\inetpub\logs\logfiles\w3svc1 will retry' error.
rootCause: IIS log directory doesn't exist at the expected path or has incorrect permissions. IIS may not be configured, the site may use a custom log path, or the log files may not be in W3C format.
solution: Validate IIS log files exist locally. Check IIS Manager for the actual log file path. Ensure the AMA agent has read access to the IIS log directory. Verify logs are in W3C Extended Log File Format.

symptom: AMA Windows performance counters missing in Perf table. MAEventTable shows PDH API errors (level 1/2/3 events).
rootCause: Counter name in DCR doesn't match the actual counter name available on the OS. Counter names are OS-locale-dependent and must exactly match what typeperf reports.
solution: Run 'typeperf -q' to list available counters on the system. Compare with counter names in the DCR/mcsconfig.latest.xml. Update the DCR to use the exact counter names as reported by typeperf. For locale-specific counter names, ensure the DCR uses the correct language variant.

symptom: Ingestion sampling is configured in Azure portal but does not take effect; telemetry volume remains unchanged
rootCause: Ingestion sampling does not apply when client-side SDK sampling (adaptive or fixed-rate) is already enabled. SDK sampling takes priority.
solution: Check if SDK-level sampling is configured in application code or config. If SDK sampling is enabled, ingestion sampling will be ignored. Remove SDK sampling first if ingestion sampling is desired.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights setup and customization/Sampling

symptom: Adaptive sampling not available or not working in .NET OpenTelemetry SDK for Application Insights
rootCause: Adaptive sampling has been removed from OpenTelemetry for .NET. Only Fixed-Rate and Ingestion sampling remain. Java still supports Rate-limited (similar to Adaptive), Fixed-Rate, and Ingestion. Python and Node.js only offer Fixed-Rate and Ingestion.
solution: For .NET OpenTelemetry: use Fixed-Rate sampling or Ingestion sampling instead of Adaptive. For Java: Rate-limited sampling provides similar behavior to Adaptive. See OpenTelemetry sampling docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/app/opentelemetry-sampling
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights setup and customization/Sampling

symptom: Security scanner flags ai_user and ai_session Application Insights cookies as vulnerable because they are not marked as HttpOnly
rootCause: The ai_user and ai_session cookies are required to be readable by the JavaScript SDK within the browser. They cannot be set as HttpOnly because the client-side SDK must read them to track user sessions.
solution: This is by design. These cookies must be readable by JavaScript. Unsupported workaround documented at: https://github.com/microsoft/ApplicationInsights-JS/issues/626
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights setup and customization/Browser telemetry

symptom: HTTP 500 or 500.53 errors after enabling APPINSIGHTS_JAVASCRIPT_ENABLED app setting for Application Insights client-side monitoring
rootCause: Conflict between Application Insights JavaScript auto-injection and URL compression (urlCompression) in IIS/App Service. The auto-injection modifies HTML responses which conflicts with compressed content.
solution: Disable URL compression or use manual JavaScript SDK injection instead of the APPINSIGHTS_JAVASCRIPT_ENABLED app setting. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/azure-web-apps-net#appinsights_javascript_enabled-and-urlcompression-is-not-supported
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights setup and customization/Browser telemetry

symptom: Performance counters not collected on Linux environment for Application Insights
rootCause: Performance counters only apply to Windows. Linux does not support Windows performance counters. Metrics (custom metrics, JMX metrics) work on both platforms.
solution: Use Metrics instead of Performance Counters for Linux environments. For Java on Linux, use JMX metrics. For .NET Core on Linux, use EventCounters or custom metrics.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights setup and customization/Metrics and performance counters

symptom: Manually-added performance counter not collected by Application Insights despite other counters working
rootCause: Counter name may be incorrect, counter doesn't exist on the system, or the counter is corrupt and not visible in Windows Performance Monitor.
solution: 1) Use Get-Counter PowerShell to validate counter exists and name is correct. 2) Check if counter is visible in Windows Performance Monitor (perfmon). 3) If counter not visible in perfmon, it may be corrupt — engage Windows Performance team or the service responsible for the counter.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights setup and customization/Metrics and performance counters

symptom: Custom log table (V1, Data Collector API) gets automatically re-created after customer deletes it
rootCause: Customer's application or pipeline is still sending data via Data Collector API. Custom log tables are implicitly re-created when data is ingested to them.
solution: Inform customer they must STOP ingesting data to the table before deleting it. Simply deleting the table is insufficient — the ingestion source must be disabled first to prevent implicit re-creation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Common Concepts/Custom logs management

symptom: Customer still charged for retention after deleting a custom log table
rootCause: Upon custom log deletion: (1) table definition removed immediately, (2) data is NOT deleted, (3) table sub-resources (including retention) are soft-deleted and hard-deleted after 14 days, (4) after hard delete, retention defaults to workspace retention — so customer is charged for data retention at workspace level.
solution: Before deleting the custom table, set table retention to minimum (4 days), then immediately delete the table. Data will be removed after 4 days and no further retention charges apply. Do NOT use data purge for cost purposes — it's meant for GDPR, can take 30 days, and does not affect retention costs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Common Concepts/Custom logs management

symptom: Custom log V1 table unexpectedly migrated to V2 and cannot be reverted
rootCause: If a V1 custom log's schema is edited via the 'Tables' blade → 'Edit schema' in Azure Portal, the table is automatically and irreversibly migrated to V2. This is by design.
solution: Inform customer this is expected behavior. The migration is irreversible. Note: this only changes the table object type, not the ingestion pipeline — data still flows via the original method until the customer explicitly switches to DCR-based ingestion.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Common Concepts/Custom logs management

symptom: DCR validation failure when creating custom log table or Data Collection Rule
rootCause: Custom column names or DCR input stream field names contain special characters, exceed 45 characters, don't start with a letter, or use reserved names (_ResourceId, id, _SubscriptionId, TenantId, Type, UniqueId, Title). Unconventional input stream field names in DCR lead to validation failures.
solution: Rename variables at source to comply with naming rules: must start with a letter, max 45 alphanumeric characters and underscores, no special characters, avoid reserved names. The _CL suffix is auto-added via portal but must be manually added via API.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Common Concepts/Custom logs management

symptom: DCR update or creation fails because transformkql parse command has more than 10 columns
rootCause: The parse command in transformkql is limited to 10 columns per statement due to performance considerations. Since Jan 6 2025, this is enforced for new/updated DCRs. By Jan 6 2026, existing DCRs with >10 elements will be stopped.
solution: Split the parse command into multiple statements, each with ≤10 columns. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/query-optimization#break-up-large-parse-commands
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Common Concepts/Ingestion Pipeline/Ingestion-time Transformation

symptom: TimeGenerated values differ between Log Analytics workspace query results and exported data (Event Hub/Storage Account/ADX), causing customer to think data export is missing records
rootCause: Log Analytics pipeline adjusts TimeGenerated if the value is >2 days before received time or >1 day in the future, replacing it with _TimeReceived. This adjustment happens downstream AFTER data export, so exported data retains the original TimeGenerated value.
solution: Use data collection transformation to preserve original TimeGenerated: add 'source | extend TimeGenerated_original = TimeGenerated' as a transformation. Then compare using TimeGenerated_original column. Product Group is working on preserving this natively (tracking: msazure/One/_workitems/edit/26993192).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Common Concepts/Ingestion Pipeline/TimeGenerated adjustments on Data Export

symptom: Error 'plugin python: plugin is disabled' (or similar for csharp, sql_request, http_request, etc.) when running KQL query in Log Analytics
rootCause: Multiple KQL plugins are intentionally disabled in Log Analytics by design. Disabled plugins include: python, csharp, r, sql_request, http_request, http_request_post, cosmosdb_sql_request, execute_query, execute_show_command, geneva_metrics_request, identity, debug_delay, infer_storage_schema, list_storage_artifacts, rows_near, azure_digital_twins_query_request, ai_embed_text.
solution: Inform customer this is by design. These plugins are disabled in Log Analytics for security and performance reasons. Some (like geneva_metrics_request) are internal. If customer needs plugin functionality, they may need to use Azure Data Explorer (ADX) directly instead of Log Analytics.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Common Concepts/Internal Guidance on Query-Related Plugin Limitations in Log Analytics

symptom: Error 'You have reached the selection limit of 5 resources' when selecting query packs in Log Analytics, even though customer has fewer than 5 selected
rootCause: The subscription scope filter may be too narrow, hiding some already-selected query packs from view. The 5 query pack limit per workspace is a performance limitation to prevent slow blade loading.
solution: Change the scope to 'Show all subscriptions' in the query pack selector view. This reveals all selected query packs across subscriptions, allowing proper deselection. The 5-pack limit is by design.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Common Concepts/Query Packs

symptom: Resource scope query returns 400 Bad Request error in Log Analytics
rootCause: Resource scope queries require records with matching _ResourceId values in an accessible workspace. If no records exist with the resource's _ResourceId, or the workspace is not accessible, the query returns 400. For subscription/RG scope, example queries may also return 400 if the query type doesn't match available data.
solution: Verify: (1) records in the workspace have _ResourceId populated matching the target resource, (2) user has access to the workspace(s) containing those records, (3) if cross-tenant, configure Azure Lighthouse. A 400 with 0 results is expected behavior when no matching data exists.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Common Concepts/Resource scope queries

symptom: Cross-tenant resource scope query stopped returning data after January 2024
rootCause: By design change introduced end of January 2024: workspaces with 'Use resource or workspace permissions' no longer allow cross-tenant data access by default. If resource is in tenant X but data is in workspace in tenant Y, and the workspace's subscription is not available from tenant X, data won't be returned.
solution: Configure Azure Lighthouse so the workspace's subscription is available in the querying tenant. Verify using Draft telemetry: look for messages 'Workspace is filtered out due to cross tenant data pollution' and 'Subscription is unauthorized and filtered out due to cross tenant pollution'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Common Concepts/Resource scope queries

symptom: Custom log table query returns 400 Bad Request when run in resource context (e.g., from a specific Azure resource's Logs blade)
rootCause: Resource context queries are only supported for specific resource types: subscription, resourceGroup, microsoft.web/sites, microsoft.compute/virtualmachines, microsoft.compute/virtualmachinescalesets. Running queries on custom logs in resource context of other types is not supported. AzureNetworkAnalytics_CL and ContainerInstanceLog_CL are common examples.
solution: Inform customer this is not supported. They should run the query in workspace context instead (navigate to Log Analytics workspace → Logs). For AzureNetworkAnalytics_CL specifically, Traffic Analytics does not emit _ResourceId values, making resource scope impossible.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Common Concepts/Resource scope queries

solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-e-query-limits.md

rootCause: Customer application still sending data via Data Collector API. Custom log tables implicitly re-created when data ingested.
solution: Must STOP ingesting data before deleting table. Deletion alone is insufficient - ingestion source must be disabled first.

symptom: Customer still charged for retention after deleting a custom log table
rootCause: Table definition removed immediately but data NOT deleted. Sub-resources (retention) soft-deleted, hard-deleted after 14 days. After hard delete, retention defaults to workspace retention.
solution: Before deleting: set table retention to minimum (4 days), then delete. Data removed after 4 days. Do NOT use data purge for cost (GDPR only, 30 days, no retention cost impact).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Common Concepts/Custom logs management

symptom: Custom log V1 table unexpectedly migrated to V2 and cannot be reverted
rootCause: Editing V1 custom log schema via Tables blade Edit schema in Azure Portal automatically and irreversibly migrates to V2. By design.
solution: Expected behavior. Migration is irreversible. Only changes table object type, not ingestion pipeline.

symptom: DCR validation failure when creating custom log table or Data Collection Rule
rootCause: Column names or DCR input stream field names contain special chars, exceed 45 chars, do not start with letter, or use reserved names (_ResourceId, id, _SubscriptionId, TenantId, Type, UniqueId, Title).
solution: Rename variables at source: start with letter, max 45 alphanumeric + underscores, no special chars, avoid reserved names. _CL suffix auto-added via portal, manual via API.

symptom: DCR update/creation fails because transformkql parse command has more than 10 columns
rootCause: parse command in transformkql limited to 10 columns per statement. Enforced for new/updated DCRs since Jan 6 2025. Existing DCRs with >10 elements stopped by Jan 6 2026.
solution: Split parse command into multiple statements with <=10 columns. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/query-optimization#break-up-large-parse-commands
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Common Concepts/Ingestion Pipeline/Ingestion-time Transformation

symptom: TimeGenerated values differ between Log Analytics workspace and exported data (Event Hub/Storage/ADX), customer thinks export missing records
rootCause: LA pipeline adjusts TimeGenerated if >2 days before received time or >1 day in future, replacing with _TimeReceived. Happens AFTER data export, so exported data retains original value.
solution: Use transformation: source | extend TimeGenerated_original = TimeGenerated. Compare using TimeGenerated_original. PG tracking native fix: msazure/One/_workitems/edit/26993192.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Common Concepts/Ingestion Pipeline/TimeGenerated adjustments on Data Export

symptom: Error plugin python: plugin is disabled (or csharp, sql_request, http_request etc) in Log Analytics KQL query
rootCause: Multiple KQL plugins intentionally disabled in LA: python, csharp, r, sql_request, http_request, http_request_post, cosmosdb_sql_request, execute_query, execute_show_command, geneva_metrics_request, identity, debug_delay, infer_storage_schema, list_storage_artifacts, rows_near, azure_digital_twins_query_request, ai_embed_text.
solution: By design for security/performance. Some plugins (geneva_metrics_request) are internal. If plugin functionality needed, use Azure Data Explorer (ADX) directly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Common Concepts/Internal Guidance on Query-Related Plugin Limitations in Log Analytics

symptom: Error You have reached the selection limit of 5 resources when selecting query packs, even with fewer than 5 selected
rootCause: Subscription scope filter too narrow, hiding already-selected query packs. 5 query pack limit per workspace is performance limitation.
solution: Change scope to Show all subscriptions in query pack selector. Reveals all selected packs. 5-pack limit is by design.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Common Concepts/Query Packs

symptom: Resource scope query returns 400 Bad Request error in Log Analytics
rootCause: Resource scope queries require records with matching _ResourceId in accessible workspace. No matching records or inaccessible workspace returns 400.
solution: Verify: (1) records have _ResourceId matching target resource, (2) user has workspace access, (3) if cross-tenant configure Azure Lighthouse. 400 with 0 results is expected when no data matches.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Common Concepts/Resource scope queries

symptom: Cross-tenant resource scope query stopped returning data after January 2024
rootCause: Design change end Jan 2024: workspaces with Use resource or workspace permissions no longer allow cross-tenant data access by default.
solution: Configure Azure Lighthouse so workspace subscription available in querying tenant. Diagnose via Draft telemetry: look for cross tenant data pollution messages.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Common Concepts/Resource scope queries

symptom: Custom log table query returns 400 Bad Request in resource context (from Azure resource Logs blade)
rootCause: Resource context queries only supported for: subscription, resourceGroup, microsoft.web/sites, microsoft.compute/virtualmachines, microsoft.compute/virtualmachinescalesets. Other types not supported.
solution: Not supported. Run query in workspace context instead. AzureNetworkAnalytics_CL specifically never emits _ResourceId.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Common Concepts/Resource scope queries

symptom: Syslog data not collected by OMS Agent on Linux — syslog events do not appear in Log Analytics workspace. Multi-step diagnostic covering heartbeat check, workspace syslog category config, DSC config refresh, rsyslog/syslog-ng conf validation, OMS Agent log verification, and syslog.conf port/IP check.
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-e-troubleshooting-syslog-collection-on-linux.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Agent Configurations/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Syslog Collection on Linux

symptom: AMA Linux extension installation fails or stucks in transitioning state on SUSE/SLES 15; insserv-compat package missing error in extension logs
rootCause: Starting from AMA 1.14.5, AMA Linux Extension requires insserv-compat package on SUSE/SLES 15. Installation script fails when SUSE repo is missing or misconfigured
solution: 1. Configure official SUSE repo on the machine. 2. Consult Linux Ninjas via collab or swarming channel. PG work item: https://msazure.visualstudio.com/One/_workitems/edit/12416349
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) for Linux/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting AMA Linux installation on SUSE (SLES) 15 fails with insserv-compat package missing

symptom: AMA/OMS Linux extension fails with [ExtensionOperationError] Non-zero exit code: 126, Permission denied on shim.sh; unable to install, uninstall or disable any extensions
rootCause: /var/ partition mounted with noexec flag prevents execution of extension scripts under /var/lib/waagent/
solution: Remove noexec flag from /etc/fstab for /var/ and remount: `mount -o remount /var` or `systemctl daemon-reload`. Verify: `findmnt -T /var | grep noexec`. AMA supports CIS lvl1/2 but noexec on /var is not standard Azure marketplace config
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) for Linux/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Unable to remove extension with error: [ExtensionOperationError] Non-zero exit code: 126

symptom: AMA Linux fails to get MSI token; extension stuck in transitioning or data missing. Errors in mdsd.err: Failed to get MSI token from IMDS endpoint http://169.254.169.254 or Enable failed with urlopen error Connection timed out
rootCause: IMDS endpoint (169.254.169.254) not reachable from the VM due to missing route, firewall rules, or network misconfiguration
solution: 1. Test IMDS: curl -s -H Metadata:true --noproxy '*' http://169.254.169.254/metadata/instance?api-version=2021-02-01 | jq. 2. Check route: netstat -r for 169.254.169.254. 3. If missing, add route: route add 169.254.169.254/32 <gateway-ip> metric 1 -p. Consult Azure VM Team
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) for Linux/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting AMA Linux - IMDS not accessible

symptom: AMA Linux JSON log collection stops working after upgrading to AMA Linux 1.31.0+ (Windows 1.25.0+); RawData column empty, data not parsed into custom columns
rootCause: Breaking change from preview to GA: JSON logs now auto-parsed into columns matching JSON tags via amaparser, no longer pushed to RawData. Portal UI DCR still hard-codes schema to TimeGenerated+RawData
solution: Use ARM template with table schema matching JSON tags instead of portal-created DCR. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/agents/data-collection-log-json?tabs=arm#incoming-stream
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) for Linux/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting AMA - Linux JSON Logs Collection

symptom: AMA Linux extension stuck in Failed or Transitioning state; cannot uninstall via Azure portal (version 1.15.2+)
rootCause: Extension processes not properly stopping during uninstall; various causes including waagent issues or partial previous installation
solution: 1. Stop all AMA processes: mdsd, mdsdmgr, telegraf, metrics-extension, fluent-bit, amacoreagent, agentlauncher. 2. CLI uninstall: az vm extension delete -g <rg> --vm-name <vm> -n Microsoft.Azure.Monitor.AzureMonitorLinuxAgent. 3. Verify removal under /opt/microsoft/azuremonitoragent/
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) for Linux/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting AMA Extension not uninstalling (1.15.2 and higher)

symptom: AMA Linux extension fails to uninstall via Portal/PowerShell/CLI with error 'Non-zero exit code: 5' and 'Unit azuremonitoragentmgr.service could not be found'. Extension status shows Transitioning. AMA services not running.
rootCause: The azuremonitoragentmgr systemd service was removed or corrupted, preventing the disable/uninstall shim script from completing successfully.
solution: Run shim.sh -install then shim.sh -enable from /var/lib/waagent/Microsoft.Azure.Monitor.AzureMonitorLinuxAgent-<version>/ to restore agent services, then retry uninstall via portal. Ignore 'already installed' errors during shim.sh execution.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) for Linux/Known Issues/AMA Linux fails uninstall via Portal or PowerShell with error: Unit azuremonitoragentmgr.service could not be found

symptom: With AMA for Linux 1.10.7 only, mdsd process fails to start after a system reboot. Data collection stops until manually fixed.
rootCause: AMA 1.10.7 creates /run/azuremonitoragent at install time for SQL Insights bridge feature. On reboot /run is cleared by OS. Without this directory, symlink creation for default_influx.socket fails preventing mdsd from starting. Only affects 1.10.7 (not 1.10.5 or 1.12.0).
solution: Upgrade to AMA 1.10.9+ which contains the hotfix. Workaround: sudo mkdir /run/azuremonitoragent && sudo chown syslog:syslog /run/azuremonitoragent && sudo systemctl restart mdsd.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) for Linux/Known Issues/AMA for linux 1.10.7: mdsd will fail to start after a system reboot

symptom: Windows AMA does not collect Event Log events near timeframe of reboot/shutdown. Shutdown/logoff events not in Log Analytics. MaEventTable.csv shows 'Table major corruption detected'.
rootCause: Non-graceful shutdown corrupts AMA cache files at C:\ProgramData\Azure Monitor Agent\AzureMonitorAgent.MonitoringDataStore\Tables. On restart AMA detects corruption, deletes and recreates tables, losing cached events not yet uploaded.
solution: Ensure reboots/shutdowns are graceful. Do not force restart (e.g. holding power button). Graceful shutdown allows cache to be properly written and closed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) for Windows/Known Issues/Azure Monitor Agent for Windows missing events near time of shutdown

symptom: After AMA installation on Azure-based Microsoft Failover Clusters (Windows), agent installs successfully but no agent processes run. ExtensionHealth log shows IMDS endpoint not found. Invoke-RestMethod to 169.254.169.254 fails.
rootCause: Microsoft Failover Cluster uses subnet 169.254.0.0/16 for internal cluster communication (Failover Cluster Virtual Adapter), conflicting with IMDS endpoint 169.254.169.254. IMDS requests routed to cluster adapter instead of wireserver.
solution: Add persistent static route on each cluster node: 'route -p add 169.254.169.254 MASK 255.255.255.255 <gatewayIP>'. Find gateway IP via 'ipconfig /all'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) for Windows/Known Issues/KI : AMA Agent is unable to start on Azure Based Microsoft Clusters - Windows Agent

symptom: SQL ATP alerts stop being generated after upgrading to AMA v1.25.0. Alert notifications to MDC fail. Possible billing anomalies for SQL services.
rootCause: AMA v1.25.0 changed encoding of resource IDs in ODS request headers. SQL ATP code cannot handle encoded resource IDs, causing failures in alert notifications to MDC and billing events.
solution: Revert AMA to version 1.24.0 as temporary workaround. See ADO wiki: How-To-force-install-previous-version-of-agent-extension-via-Azure-PowerShell. Wait for PG patch.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) for Windows/Known Issues/SQL ATP Issue with AMA 1.25.0

symptom: AMA Linux extension shows Failed/Transitioning status and cannot be uninstalled normally through portal or CLI (version 1.15.2 and higher).
rootCause: Extension stuck in bad provisioning state preventing normal uninstallation flow through Azure platform.
solution: Manual removal: 1) Stop all agent processes (mdsd, mdsdmgr, telegraf, metrics-extension, fluent-bit, amacoreagent, agentlauncher). 2) Run 'az vm extension delete -g <rg> --vm-name <vm> -n Microsoft.Azure.Monitor.AzureMonitorLinuxAgent'. Verify /etc/default/azuremonitoragent, /etc/opt/microsoft/azuremonitoragent, /opt/microsoft/azuremonitoragent are deleted.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) for Linux/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting AMA Extension not uninstalling (1.15.2 and higher)

symptom: AMA Linux cannot get MSI token because IMDS (169.254.169.254) unreachable. Before install: extension stuck Transitioning with 'Enable failed: urlopen error Connection timed out'. After install: extension shows succeeded but mdsd.err shows 'Failed to get MSI token from IMDS endpoint', no data in destinations.
rootCause: No network route to IMDS IP 169.254.169.254, or network configuration (firewall/custom routing/NSG) blocking access to IMDS endpoint on the VM.
solution: Step 1: Test IMDS with curl -s -H Metadata:true --noproxy '*' 'http://169.254.169.254/metadata/instance?api-version=2021-02-01' | jq. Step 2: Check routes with netstat -r. If no route for 169.254.169.254, add route: route add 169.254.169.254/32 <gateway_ip> metric 1 -p. Engage Azure VM team if still failing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) for Linux/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting AMA Linux - IMDS not accessible

symptom: JavaScript SDK telemetry missing or blocked; HAR trace shows track calls with no Response Headers, or POST with no Payload; client-side telemetry stops working or never works
rootCause: CORS blocked by browser (e.g., Edge) due to tracking policy; custom domain not included in AllowTrackingForUrls browser policy list, preventing CORS preflight from completing
solution: Configure Edge browser policy AllowTrackingForUrls to include Application Insights ingestion endpoint and any custom domain names used by the app. Roll out policy change via group policy. Note: this is one possible cause — many other browser/network causes exist.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Additional Reference Material/SDK and Agents References/Validate javascript functionality
