symptom: AMPLS - VMs on VNET cannot resolve App Insights / Log Analytics / Azure Monitor endpoints after removing resource from Azure Monitor Private Link Scope (AMPLS). DNS resolution returns 'domain not found' for privatelink.monitor.azure.com domains.
rootCause: When removing AI/LA from AMPLS, A-Records in Private DNS Zones are deleted but the Private DNS Zones themselves remain linked to the VNET. Private clients query the empty Private DNS Zone which returns 'not found' instead of falling through to public DNS.
solution: Three options: (1) Delete Virtual Network Links and/or Private DNS Zones (only if last AMPLS resource); (2) Add HOSTS file entries mapping endpoints to public IPs; (3) Manually add CNAME records in Private DNS Zones pointing to public Traffic Manager profiles. Option 3 requires ongoing maintenance as TM profile names may change.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/54404

symptom: Application Insights component in West Central US (westcentralus) cannot query logs, shows missing data. Customers may also be unable to delete, modify or create new App Insights resources in this region.
rootCause: West Central US is an internal-only 'AIMON' region accidentally exposed publicly during onboarding (April-May 2021). OBO service calls control plane to convert AppId to WorkspaceId but gets 404 since region doesn't hold knowledge of public resource ikeys/appids, silently dropping data.
solution: Inform customer WCU is not supported for public AI resources. Create new AI resource in another region, update connection string in applications. For cleanup, get customer confirmation then open CRI to CDS Control Plane team to manually delete the rogue WCU component (portal delete will fail).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/56388

symptom: Daily cap warning threshold alerts/emails never fire for workspace-based Application Insights components. Only the final daily cap reached email arrives, but the percentage-based warning threshold email does not.
rootCause: The 'Daily cap warning level' threshold notification was never implemented for workspace-based AI components. It only works for Classic Application Insights instances.
solution: Workaround: Create a log query scheduled alert that monitors ingestion volume against the configured threshold. A PowerShell script (ThresholdAlerts.ps1) can automate creating action groups and scheduled query alerts for all workspace-based AI components in a subscription. The script uses _BilledSize to compute daily ingestion volume and triggers when exceeding the warning threshold percentage.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/56627

symptom: Customer wants to move Application Insights resource to a different Azure region. Move operation is not available.
rootCause: Cross-region move for Application Insights components is not supported by design. See microsoft.insights/components in the Azure Resource Manager move support matrix.
solution: Create a new Application Insights resource in the target region and swap instrumentation keys/connection strings. Refer to the public FAQ 'How do I move an Application Insights resource to a new region?' and the internal wiki page on Swap Instrumentation Keys.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/58109

symptom: Diagnostic settings blade is missing under the Monitoring tab for an Application Insights resource in Azure Portal.
rootCause: The AI resource is a classic (non-workspace-based) type. Classic Application Insights resources do not show the Diagnostic Settings blade.
solution: Migrate the classic Application Insights resource to workspace-based using the migration guide: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/app/convert-classic-resource#migrate-your-resource
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/59209

symptom: Trace/log items appear duplicated 3-4 times in the Application Insights traces table for Java apps running on Dell Boomi platform. BOOMI logs themselves do not show duplication.
rootCause: By design (external). The Boomi platform's default logging behavior causes repeated trace emissions to Application Insights.
solution: Set the 'Write Once Per Execution' flag to true in the Boomi configuration. Related ICM: 411078017.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/61601

symptom: Customer reports data latency query shows telemetry took 2+ days (sometimes 8 days) to be ingested into Application Insights / Log Analytics. Note: AI does NOT ingest data older than 48 hours, so 3+ day latency always indicates this issue.
rootCause: Internal KCM service moves App Insights data between Kusto databases (e.g., for service healing). This data movement operation resets the ingestion_time() value, making latency calculations appear artificially inflated. All moved records will show identical ingestion_time().
solution: No fix available. Inform customer that ingestion_time() was reset by internal data movement and their latency queries are inaccurate for those records. New data ingestion is not affected unless future data movement occurs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/61748

symptom: 'Profile Now' button in Application Insights Profiler fails instantly with 'Profiling Failed. Refresh and try again.' error when AI resource is in a closed Private Link Scope (public querying disabled). Automatic profiling works fine. Browser shows 403 from gateway.azureserviceprofiler.net with message 'This resource must be accessed via Private Link'.
rootCause: An internal configuration change broke Private Link IP address resolution for the 'gateway.azureserviceprofiler.net' hostname, which resolves to the 'diagservices-query' DNS Zone entry in AMPLS. The profiler gateway cannot be reached via Private Link for on-demand profiling. Related ICM: 440629501.
solution: Workaround: Enable public network access for 'Query' in the App Insights resource's 'Network Isolation' tab. This allows the Profile Now button to reach the profiler gateway through public endpoint. A permanent fix was planned before March 2024.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/62823

symptom: Node.js Application Insights SDK does not fully integrate with Next.js 13+ App Router model. Auto-collected dependencies (e.g., Azure SDK calls) are not tracked. Some SDK functionalities don't behave as expected.
rootCause: The Node.js SDK's diagnostic-channel-publishers patching mechanism has compatibility issues with Next.js App Router's initialization model. Exact root cause unclear but related to how Next.js and Node.js initialize together under the App Router paradigm.
solution: PG was planning a sample/cleaner integration path for Next.js App Router + Node.js SDK (estimated end of Jan 2024, may be delayed). Work with SME/TA/Manager for appropriate customer communication. Related ICM: 432003586.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/62914

symptom: Data Retention button on 'Usage and estimated costs' blade remains enabled/visible for a workspace-based Application Insights component that was migrated from classic.
rootCause: By design. Migrated classic AI components still retain historical telemetry in the original underlying classic resource. The Data Retention button controls retention for that legacy data store.
solution: No action needed. Wait for all historical data in the classic resource to age out based on the configured retention period. Once aged out, the Data Retention button will automatically become disabled, and all retention is controlled from the Log Analytics workspace.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/63575

symptom: High number of failures with 4xx result codes (e.g. 404) on Application Insights Failures blade, typically on operations like 'GET /' or 'GET /admin/host/ping'. Requests appear at regular intervals (every 5-15 minutes), source IP is 127.0.0.1, and operation_SyntheticSource may show 'Bot'.
rootCause: App Service features like Always On, Health Check, or Automatic Scaling trigger HTTP requests to the application root path or '/admin/host/ping'. If the app (often a Web API) is not configured to respond to these paths, 404 errors are generated. Application Insights SDK simply logs these requests — it is not the source of the errors.
solution: 1) Identify the source: check if Always On, Health Check, or Automatic Scaling is enabled. 2) Disable Always On or configure Health Check with a specific valid path instead of root. 3) For Automatic Scaling, requests go to '/admin/host/ping' — this is by design. 4) Alternatively, use telemetry processors or data collection transformations to filter out the 4xx responses. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/app/api-filtering-sampling
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/50100

symptom: Telemetry stops showing or disappears from a workspace-based Application Insights component. Overview pane may display a banner indicating the workspace is no longer valid; other panes (Performance, etc.) show no data.
rootCause: The Log Analytics workspace that the Application Insights component is pointed to is no longer valid — it was either deleted or moved to a different resource group.
solution: 1) Go to the Application Insights Properties pane, scroll to bottom and verify the workspace reference. 2) If the workspace was deleted: recover it via https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/delete-workspace 3) If moved to another resource group: update the workspace reference. Note: any telemetry sent while pointing to an invalid workspace is lost and cannot be recovered.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/50424

symptom: Visual Studio CodeLens method annotations for Application Insights show zero '0' values for request count and exception rate and never update, despite the application producing telemetry.
rootCause: Two issues: 1) The VS extension's KQL queries contain '\r\n' newline characters that Draft fails to deserialize (returns BadArgumentError/InvalidJsonBody). 2) The ApplicationInsightsAnnotationResourceId XML element may be missing from the .csproj file — it is not added automatically by Connected Services.
solution: 1) Add both <ApplicationInsightsResourceId> and <ApplicationInsightsAnnotationResourceId> elements inside <PropertyGroup> in the .csproj file with the full resource ID path. 2) The \r\n query issue was fixed in Visual Studio 17.7-preview2 (GA August 2023) — ensure VS is updated. Related VS bug: https://dev.azure.com/devdiv/DevDiv/_workitems/edit/1818076
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/50460

symptom: Transaction Search in Application Insights shows only limited telemetry types. ASC Ingestion chart confirms only traces, custom events, and custom metrics are being received, with old SDK versions like 'w_ap:2.6.2010.2703', 'a_sc:1.3.7.5', 'dotnet:2.6.4-28384'.
rootCause: The application's Application Insights SDK is not sending telemetry. The limited data shown is from Profiler and Snapshot Debugger, which use old SDK versions that cannot be changed. These are not representative of application telemetry.
solution: 1) Verify the SDK versions using ASC Ingestion tab (Aggregate by Raw SDK Names) or query: union AppEvents, AppTraces, AppMetrics | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d) | distinct Name, SDKVersion. 2) If only Profiler/Snapshot Debugger SDKs are present, the issue is with the application's SDK instrumentation — investigate why the app SDK is not sending telemetry. 3) Set proper SAP: 'Missing All Telemetry' or 'Missing Specific Telemetry Types'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/52029

symptom: Node.js App Service with Application Insights auto-instrumentation shows '__Empty' response codes on the Failures blade. Clicking the __Empty line returns no telemetry items. The __Empty response code cannot be found in request logs.
rootCause: The Node.js SDK pre-aggregated metrics tallies requests where the client disconnects/times out/aborts before the server returns a response code as '__Empty'. This value feeds MDM which powers the Failures blade. The underlying telemetry items are rejected by Application Insights ingestion because they lack a valid response code.
solution: Disable pre-aggregated metrics using JSON configuration: set 'enableAutoCollectPreAggregatedMetrics: false'. Configure via: 1) Create a JSON config file and set APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONFIGURATION_FILE env var to its path, or 2) Pass JSON string directly in APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONFIGURATION_CONTENT env var. See: https://github.com/microsoft/ApplicationInsights-node.js#configuration. Related GitHub issue: https://github.com/microsoft/ApplicationInsights-node.js/issues/782
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/54136

symptom: Live Metrics stopped working for apps using ApplicationInsights-node.js 2.9.0; 'Could not connect to your application' message on Live Metrics blade
rootCause: Bug in Live Metrics enablement shipped with Node.JS SDK version 2.9.0
solution: Update to Node.JS SDK 2.9.1 which fixes the Live Metrics initialization issue
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/64488

symptom: End-to-End Transaction Details shows hundreds/thousands of unrelated telemetry items (requests, dependencies, exceptions, traces) associated with same operation ID, spanning hours apart
rootCause: APIM default correlation protocol is 'Legacy', using Request-Id header which may generate non-unique operation IDs when inbound requests lack this header
solution: Set APIM telemetry correlation protocol setting to 'None'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/64614

symptom: Adding Azure Monitor resource to AMPLS exhausts subnet IPs; provisioning gets stuck then fails after ~25 min; all connected resources show 'Failed' provisioning state; all telemetry stops flowing with 403 invalid private link id
rootCause: Subnet too small (e.g. /27) to accommodate all required DNS-to-IP mappings for AMPLS resources; control plane bug between AMPLS, CDS and NRP teams causes complete AMPLS breakage when IP exhaustion occurs
solution: 1) Remove the resource that caused IP exhaustion; 2) Add a larger subnet (e.g. /26 with 59 available IPs); 3) Delete and recreate Private Endpoint selecting the larger subnet
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/64682

symptom: Adding resource to AMPLS while previous resource still provisioning breaks all existing Private Endpoint connections; all Azure Monitor resources connected to AMPLS show 'Failed' state and telemetry drops
rootCause: NRP recreate private endpoints job uses different configuration than current AMPLS state during parallel provisioning; timing bug causes PE to become unhealthy
solution: Add or remove any resource from the AMPLS to trigger a 'refresh' that gives the NRP recreate job a second chance to succeed and restore connections
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/64682

symptom: Comparing telemetry data against external log sources (HTTP logs, custom logs) shows random small percentage of records missing in Application Insights requests/traces tables on some days
rootCause: By design — Application Insights telemetry channels do not guarantee 100% delivery; see MS Learn docs on telemetry channel delivery guarantees
solution: No fix available; expected behavior if loss is within a small percentage. Investigate on case-by-case basis; set expectations early with customer
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/65260

symptom: Azure Function with auto-attach to App Insights randomly stops ingesting telemetry; ingestion metrics show telemetry dropped with 'e' (wrong endpoint) status code
rootCause: TelemetryConfiguration has mismatched Instrumentation Key vs Connection String ikey values; Function App randomly toggles between them, causing 307 redirect to wrong region; SDK caches redirected endpoint URL for 12+ hours, subsequent requests with correct ikey get 400 Bad Request
solution: Delete APPINSIGHTS_INSTRUMENTATIONKEY setting and only use APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING. Verify ikey values match in both settings. Check for PnP Core SDK conflict and disable its telemetry (PNP-DISABLETELEMETRY=true) if present. Restart function app for immediate mitigation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/65271

symptom: ASP.NET Core app with auto-instrumentation enabled shows Application Insights as enabled but no telemetry gets collected; attach status logs show StartupHook initializes but no AspNetCore-StartupBootstrapper events
rootCause: Customer code calls WebHostDefaults.HostingStartupAssembliesKey with custom assembly list that does not include Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.StartupBootstrapper; this overwrites the ASPNETCORE_HOSTINGSTARTUPASSEMBLIES environment variable, preventing App Insights SDK from loading
solution: Append the original ASPNETCORE_HOSTINGSTARTUPASSEMBLIES env var value to the custom startup assemblies list: .UseSetting(WebHostDefaults.HostingStartupAssembliesKey, string.Join(";", HostingManager.GetModules()) + Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("ASPNETCORE_HOSTINGSTARTUPASSEMBLIES"))
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/65580

symptom: Telemetry ingestion fails on workspace-based Application Insights components; IngestionMode property shown as 'Disabled'; ASC ingestion tab shows 'WK' error codes
rootCause: Broken/outdated ARM reference to associated Log Analytics workspace, usually after accidentally deleting and recreating the workspace with same name but different workspace ID
solution: If workspace in soft-delete state, recover it to restore ingestion. If permanently deleted and recreated with same name, link the AI component to a different workspace and then link it back to the original workspace to update ARM references
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/65964

symptom: In .NET Core apps, telemetry from logging adapters (NLog, Log4Net, ILogger) behaves differently from App Insights SDK telemetry — not sampled even when SDK telemetry is heavily sampled, missing client IP information
rootCause: In .NET Core, logging adapters use TelemetryConfiguration.Active which creates a new TelemetryConfiguration without the same processors and initializers (AdaptiveSamplingTelemetryProcessor, ClientIpHeaderTelemetryInitializer, etc.) as the SDK's configuration; behavior change from .NET Framework
solution: Set EnableActiveTelemetryConfigurationSetup=true in ApplicationInsightsServiceOptions: builder.Services.AddApplicationInsightsTelemetry(o => { o.EnableActiveTelemetryConfigurationSetup = true; })
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/66073

symptom: App Service web app on old Application Insights private site extension version 2.6.5 (.NET Framework); SDK update doesn't work; auto-instrumentation fails
rootCause: Old private site extension (Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.AzureWebSites) no longer in use but still present on some App Services, blocking the new auto-instrumentation mechanism
solution: Delete the Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.AzureWebSites folder from D:\home\siteextensions via KUDU Debug Console. If 'Access Denied', stop the App Service first, or delete the applicationHost.xdt and scmApplicationHost.xdt files inside the folder first, then stop and retry deletion. Use AppLens 'Private SiteExtension Check for AppInsights' detector to confirm
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/66919

symptom: Failed OPTIONS preflight requests in browser dev tools to Application Insights ingestion endpoint (dc.applicationinsights.us/v2/track) when using Power BI JavaScript plugin (app.powerbigov.us); POST requests show ERR_FAILED
rootCause: CORS failure — Power BI plugin sends OPTIONS request with Origin 'https://app.powerbigov.us' but Application Insights endpoints don't respond to OPTIONS requests, returning 404 Not Found; browser blocks subsequent POST
solution: No user action needed — these failed requests don't impact the application. The telemetry calls are from the Power BI plugin's embedded code, not the customer's app. If prevention is needed, engage Power BI team
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/67745

symptom: Live Metrics stopped working after updating to ApplicationInsights-node.js 2.9.0. 'Could not connect to your application' message on Live Metrics blade.
rootCause: Bug with Live Metrics enablement shipped in Node.JS SDK version 2.9.0.
solution: Update to ApplicationInsights-node.js SDK version 2.9.1 which fixes the Live Metrics initialization issue.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/64488

symptom: Hundreds or thousands of unrelated telemetry items (requests, dependencies, exceptions, traces) associated with the same operation ID in End-to-End Transaction Details, even for distinct calls hours apart.
rootCause: APIM default correlation protocol is 'Legacy', using Request-Id HTTP header. If inbound request lacks this header, APIM generates a non-unique operation ID.
solution: Set the APIM telemetry correlation protocol to 'None' instead of 'Legacy'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/64614

symptom: Adding Azure Monitor resource to AMPLS exhausts subnet IPs, all connected resources enter Failed provisioning state. Telemetry drops with 403 'invalid private link id'. NRP logs show SubnetIsFull error.
rootCause: Bug in control plane code between AMPLS, CDS and NRP teams. Subnet IP exhaustion during provisioning corrupts all existing AMPLS connections.
solution: 1) Remove the resource that caused IP exhaustion. 2) Add a larger subnet (e.g. /26 instead of /27). 3) Delete and recreate the Private Endpoint on the larger subnet. IP needs: AI first=10 addl=2; LA first=10 addl=2; DCR first=11 addl=3.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/64682

symptom: Adding resource to AMPLS while a previous resource is still provisioning breaks existing PE connections. All scoped resources enter Failed state, telemetry stops.
rootCause: Timing bug: NRP recreates Private Endpoint with a different configuration than current AMPLS state when parallel provisioning operations overlap.
solution: Add or remove any resource from the AMPLS to trigger a 'refresh' that re-runs the recreate job. Avoid adding multiple resources simultaneously.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/64682

symptom: Random, small percentage of telemetry records missing. HTTP log request counts vs AppRequests or custom logs vs AppTraces show occasional daily discrepancies.
rootCause: By design. Application Insights telemetry channels do not guarantee 100% delivery. Small random loss is expected behavior.
solution: No fix available (expected behavior). Set expectations early with customer. Refer to 'Investigate randomly missing telemetry' and 'Intermittent or Partial Loss of Telemetry' wiki guides.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/65260

symptom: Azure Function App telemetry randomly drops with 'e' (wrong endpoint) status code in ingestion metrics. Telemetry works after restart but gaps reappear randomly.
rootCause: TelemetryConfiguration has mismatched IKey values between instrumentationKey and connectionString fields. Function App randomly toggles between them, causing 307 redirect to wrong region. SDK caches redirect URL for 12+ hours.
solution: Delete APPINSIGHTS_INSTRUMENTATIONKEY setting, only use APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING. Ensure matching ikey if both present. If using PnP Core SDK, set PNP-DISABLETELEMETRY=true. Restart for immediate mitigation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/65271

symptom: ASP.NET Core app on App Service/Function/VM with auto-instrumentation enabled but no telemetry collected. StartupHook initializes but no StartupBootstrapper events in attach status.
rootCause: App code calls WebHostDefaults.HostingStartupAssembliesKey with custom assemblies, overwriting ASPNETCORE_HOSTINGSTARTUPASSEMBLIES without including Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.StartupBootstrapper.
solution: Append original env var value to custom list: .UseSetting(WebHostDefaults.HostingStartupAssembliesKey, string.Join(";", HostingManager.GetModules()) + Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("ASPNETCORE_HOSTINGSTARTUPASSEMBLIES"))
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/65580

symptom: Workspace-based Application Insights component shows IngestionMode as 'Disabled'. Telemetry ingestion fails. ASC Ingestion tab shows WK error codes.
rootCause: Broken/outdated reference to associated Log Analytics workspace, typically from accidental workspace deletion. Recreating workspace with same name creates different workspace ID causing mismatch.
solution: If workspace in soft-delete: recover it. If permanently deleted and recreated: link AI component to a different workspace, then link back to original to refresh ARM references and restore ingestion.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/65964

symptom: .NET Core app: telemetry from logging adapters (NLog, Log4Net, ILogger) not sampled while App Insights native telemetry is sampled. Or logging adapter telemetry missing client IP information.
rootCause: .NET Core SDK behavior change: logging adapters use TelemetryConfiguration.Active which returns a new config without standard processors (AdaptiveSamplingTelemetryProcessor) and initializers (ClientIpHeaderTelemetryInitializer).
solution: Set EnableActiveTelemetryConfigurationSetup=true: builder.Services.AddApplicationInsightsTelemetry(o => { o.EnableActiveTelemetryConfigurationSetup = true; }); This populates TelemetryConfiguration.Active with same processors/initializers as main config.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/66073

symptom: App Service auto-instrumentation fails. Old Application Insights private site extension version 2.6.5 still present, preventing SDK updates.
rootCause: Outdated Application Insights private site extension (2.6.5) no longer in use but still installed in rare cases, blocking newer auto-instrumentation.
solution: Remove via KUDU: Advanced Tools > Debug Console > navigate to D:\home\siteextensions > delete 'Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.AzureWebSites'. If access denied: stop App Service first, or delete applicationHost.xdt and scmApplicationHost.xdt inside the folder. Use AppLens 'Private SiteExtension Check' detector to confirm.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/66919

symptom: Failed OPTIONS preflight requests to Application Insights ingestion endpoints (dc.applicationinsights.us/v2/track) in browser dev tools. 404 Not Found for CORS requests. Console errors: POST net::ERR_FAILED.
rootCause: Power BI JavaScript plugin (app.powerbigov.us) embeds code sending telemetry to AI ingestion endpoints. AI endpoints don't respond to OPTIONS requests, so CORS preflight fails with 404 and the POST never executes.
solution: No impact on the application - failed requests are harmless. The telemetry POST never executes due to CORS failure. If customer wants to prevent the calls, engage Power BI team. External plugin code outside customer control.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/67745

symptom: Duplicated Exception telemetry from API Management service into Application Insights. Exception telemetry becomes duplicated when APIM policy code throws C# exceptions while Application Insights integration is enabled.
rootCause: Bug in a gateway component within APIM's infrastructure that duplicates exception records (different _ItemId values in AppExceptions table).
solution: Known APIM bug. Work item Bug 18434395 exists for APIM team to fix. Reference IcM 469904134.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/68078

symptom: PowerShell Update-AzApplicationInsights cmdlet strips existing Tags from Application Insights resource when updating the component.
rootCause: Bug in Azure PowerShell code: spelling error 'AddtionalProperties' instead of 'AdditionalProperties' causes existing tags to be lost during update.
solution: Workaround: Retrieve current tags first using Get-AzApplicationInsights, store in hashtable, then pass $tagHashtable via -Tag parameter to Update-AzApplicationInsights. Azure CLI is not affected. Fix expected in next Azure PowerShell milestone.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/68086

symptom: Unexpected latency (up to 24 hours) in telemetry arriving at Application Insights from Dynamics 365 Power Platform CDS Data Export feature.
rootCause: By design: latency from D365 Power Platform CDS Data Export source can be up to 24 hours SLA, with typical delays of 30-90 minutes. Falls outside Application Insights PG scope.
solution: No resolution to improve this latency. Identify telemetry source via cloud_RoleName / AppRoleName field showing 'CDS Data Export'. Only applicable when delay is at source (client side); if ingestion pipeline delay, follow normal investigation. SAP: Dynamics/Power Platform Administration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/68452

symptom: Application Insights E2E transaction view shows empty page with message 'This transaction doesn't contain any requests, dependencies, or exceptions' despite telemetry items existing in logs for the operation ID.
rootCause: Circular reference between two telemetry items' parent IDs — neither can be determined as root parent, so the E2E Gantt chart cannot render the hierarchy.
solution: Customer must update application code to break the circular reference between telemetry items' parent-child IDs. See custom operations tracking documentation. App Insights SDKs do not produce this by default; occurs with manual tracking or manual id/parentId setting.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/69618

symptom: Live Metrics CPU Total % chart displays percentage higher than 100% (can reach 200-400+%).
rootCause: By design: Live Metrics monitors process-specific \Process(??APP_WIN32_PROC??)\% Processor Time which reports per-process CPU across all cores, while SDK monitors \Processor(_Total)\% Processor Time which is averaged. On multi-core machines, process CPU maximum = 100 * ProcessorCount.
solution: No fix, by design. Only impacts Live Metrics CPU chart. Inform customers the CPU values over 100% result from multi-core machines. OTel SDKs will have more consistent Live Metrics reporting in future.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/69742

symptom: Users column missing in Application Insights Failures pane operation name table.
rootCause: By design: the Failures blade query uses dcountif(user_Id,...) to calculate userCount. If userCount is zero (no user_Id populated in telemetry), the Users column is not displayed.
solution: Customer must produce telemetry that populates the user_Id field. Most common approach: collect browser-based client-side telemetry with Application Insights JavaScript SDK which auto-populates user_Id with default values (e.g., via PageViews telemetry).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/70423

symptom: Unable to find or add NuGet packages (including Application Insights SDK) in Visual Studio project.
rootCause: NuGet public package source (nuget.org) not configured in Visual Studio's Package Manager Settings.
solution: Go to Tools > NuGet Package Manager > Package Manager Settings > Package Sources. Add new source with URL https://api.nuget.org/v3/index.json. Save changes and restart Visual Studio. Ensure Package Source dropdown is set to 'All'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/70443

symptom: String values in dynamic fields (customDimensions/Properties) automatically converted to datetime or other scalar types in Application Insights / Log Analytics. E.g., custom string '9814-05-01' becomes datetime '9814-05-01T00:00:00.0000000Z'.
rootCause: By design: Kusto auto-parses string values in dynamic fields that match supported datetime formats (####-##-##) into strongly-typed datetime values. Dynamic field type in App Insights default tables enables this behavior.
solution: Workaround: break the regex pattern Kusto uses for auto-parsing, e.g., prefix the string value to prevent matching datetime format. Changing table field type from dynamic to string would prevent this, but App Insights team will not change default table schema. Alternatively, use tostring() in queries to cast back.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/70616

symptom: Slack CloudBot for Azure add-on fails when connecting to Application Insights resource to query telemetry using API key.
rootCause: Conflict querying telemetry from backend Log Analytics workspace. Common causes: (1) connecting to classic Application Insights resource, (2) associated LA workspace has been deleted, (3) workspace Access Control Mode set to 'Required workspace permissions' blocking API key queries.
solution: Ensure backend LA workspace exists and can accept API key queries. Set Access Control Mode to 'Use resource or workspace permissions'. If issue persists, redirect customer to CloudBot support (third-party tool, obscures REST API calls).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/70970

symptom: Application Insights Profiler on Azure Functions fails with error 'Merging of ETL files has failed (0x80070006)'. COMException at DiagnosticsHub.StandardCollector.Host.Interop.ICollectionSession.Stop. No profiling sessions recorded.
rootCause: Believed to be resource limitations on the VM hosting the Function worker, causing ETL file merge to fail during profiling session stop.
solution: Potential workaround: scale App Service plan up/down to move worker to a new VM (not always successful). Check Antares version. Profiler team awaiting Antares 102 rollout which changes merge process and enhances troubleshooting. Reference IcM 454107428.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/71664

symptom: Exception telemetry duplicated when API Management (APIM) sends telemetry to Application Insights. Occurs when C# code in APIM policies throws exceptions. Different _ItemId values for each duplicate in AppExceptions.
rootCause: Bug in APIM gateway component causes exception telemetry to be recorded twice (Bug 18434395).
solution: APIM team bug fix pending (Bug 18434395). No customer-side workaround available.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/68078

symptom: Running Update-AzApplicationInsights PowerShell cmdlet clears all existing Tags from the Application Insights resource. Tags collection wiped on every update.
rootCause: Typo in Azure PowerShell code: AddtionalProperties instead of AdditionalProperties causes Tags to be lost during update.
solution: Workaround: retrieve current Tags via Get-AzApplicationInsights, build hashtable from Tag.AdditionalProperties, pass -Tag param. Azure CLI not affected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/68086

symptom: Unexpected latency (up to 24h, typically 30-90 min) in telemetry arriving at Application Insights from D365 Power Platform CDS Data Export.
rootCause: By design. Power Platform Data Export has documented SLA of up to 24h latency. Outside App Insights PG scope.
solution: No fix. Identify source via cloud_RoleName = CDS Data Export. If server-side ingestion delay, follow normal delayed telemetry investigation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/68452

symptom: E2E transaction view (Gantt chart) shows blank page with no requests/dependencies/exceptions message, but telemetry items exist in Logs for the operation_id.
rootCause: Circular reference between two telemetry items, each points to the other as parent (operation_ParentId). E2E view cannot determine root parent.
solution: Customer must fix application code to break circular parent-child reference. SDKs do not produce this by default; occurs with manual telemetry tracking.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/69618

symptom: Live Metrics CPU Total % chart shows values exceeding 100% (200-400%+) in Application Insights portal.
rootCause: By design. Live Metrics monitors process-specific Processor Time (per-process, can exceed 100% on multi-core), while SDK monitors total average capped at 100%.
solution: No fix. Max CPU = 100 * ProcessorCount on multi-core. OTel SDKs will have more consistent reporting. Only Live Metrics CPU chart affected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/69742

symptom: Failures blade missing Users column in operation name table. Only operation_Name, sum_itemCount, failedCount shown; no userCount column.
rootCause: By design. Failures experience hides Users column when all dcountif(user_Id) values are zero in query results.
solution: Populate user_Id in telemetry. Common approach: collect browser-side telemetry with App Insights JavaScript SDK which auto-populates user_Id.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/70423

symptom: Cannot find NuGet packages (including Application Insights SDKs) in Visual Studio NuGet Package Manager. Package search returns no results.
rootCause: NuGet package source (nuget.org) not configured in Visual Studio Package Manager Settings.
solution: Add source https://api.nuget.org/v3/index.json in Tools > NuGet Package Manager > Package Sources. Save, restart VS. Set Package source to All.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/70443

symptom: String values in dynamic fields (customDimensions/Properties) auto-converted to datetime or scalar types upon ingestion. E.g., 9814-05-01 becomes 9814-05-01T00:00:00Z.
rootCause: By design. Kusto dynamic type auto-parses strings matching datetime formats into datetime scalars. Properties field in App Insights tables is dynamic type.
solution: No platform fix. Workaround: break datetime regex by adding prefix chars (e.g., A1111-22-33, id:1111-22-33) so Kusto does not parse as datetime.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/70616

symptom: Slack add-on CloudBot for Azure fails to connect to Application Insights resource and query telemetry using API key.
rootCause: Conflict querying backend LA workspace: classic AI resource, deleted workspace, or Access Control Mode set to Required workspace permissions.
solution: Ensure backend LA workspace exists. Set Access Control Mode to Use resource or workspace permissions. If persists, redirect to CloudBot support.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/70970

symptom: App Insights Profiler on Azure Functions fails: COMException Merging of ETL files has failed (0x80070006). No profiling sessions recorded.
rootCause: Resource limitations on VM hosting the Function worker, preventing ETL file merging by DiagnosticsHub StandardCollector.
solution: Workaround: scale App Service plan up/down to move worker to new VM. Check Antares version. Awaiting Antares 102 rollout. Escalate to Profiler PG if persists.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/71664

symptom: Get-ApplicationInsightsMonitoringStatus cmdlet fails with error 'GetOutOfProcInfo: The object identifier does not represent a valid object. (Exception from HRESULT: 0x800710D8)' when an FTP site is configured in IIS
rootCause: The cmdlet parses applicationHost.config to report attach status. FTP sites use a protocol binding format that the cmdlet cannot handle, causing a COMException. This is a known bug in the Status Monitor v2 cmdlet (maintenance mode, will not be fixed).
solution: Remove or temporarily rename FTP site entries from applicationHost.config before running the cmdlet. The FTP site only affects the status cmdlet - App Insights Agent still attaches and sends telemetry normally.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/72419

symptom: Get-ApplicationInsightsMonitoringStatus cmdlet fails with NullReferenceException 'Object reference not set to an instance of an object' due to malformed applicationHost.config referencing non-existent application pool
rootCause: The applicationHost.config XML file contains an <application> entry with applicationPool attribute pointing to a pool that no longer exists in IIS, causing the cmdlet XML parser to throw NullReferenceException.
solution: Validate and fix the applicationHost.config file - ensure all <application> entries reference valid application pools that exist in IIS. The cmdlet failure does not impact App Insights agent functionality.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/72419

symptom: Cloud/App Role Names randomly disappear in Application Insights Performance or Failures blades when time range exceeds 1 hour or extends beyond 24 hours, despite data being accessible in shorter time ranges
rootCause: Application Insights Standard Metrics have a hard limit of 100 unique dimension values per dimension. When a component has >100 unique cloud_RoleName values, the ingestion pipeline cannot maintain correct accuracy, causing role names to appear/disappear inconsistently across time ranges.
solution: Reduce the number of unique cloud/app role names emitting telemetry to a single Application Insights component to below 100. Distribute or offboard some applications to separate AI components. Use KQL (requests | summarize count() by bin(timestamp,1d), cloud_RoleName) to audit unique role name count.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/73189

symptom: Application Insights Profiler generates COMException 'Exception from HRESULT: 0xE111005E' on StartProfiler and InvalidOperationException 'There is no active profiling session' on StopProfiler in App Service
rootCause: Antares platform v102 changed concurrent profiling session support - only one profiling session is allowed per App Service Plan. If another app in the same plan already has an active session, new sessions are blocked with 0xE111005E (max sessions reached).
solution: No action required - exceptions do not impact application performance. A Profiler agent update will internally handle these exceptions (ETA early 2025 via Antares rapid update). Until then, exceptions are cosmetic and can be safely ignored.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/73978

symptom: Application Insights cloud_RoleInstance does not match the actual instance name (Host) on Linux-based App Service - shows WEBSITE_INSTANCE_ID instead of host name
rootCause: By design: Application Insights .NET SDK and Azure Monitor Exporter use WEBSITE_INSTANCE_ID as cloud role instance ID for App Service. Standardized behavior, not slated for modification to avoid breaking changes.
solution: Implement a Telemetry Initializer that uses the ComputerName environment variable (verified by App Service Team to expose correct Host Name for Linux App Service) to override cloud_RoleInstance value.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/75701

symptom: Data ingestion costs increase after migrating from classic Application Insights to workspace-based resource
rootCause: Migration from classic resource under basic (non-regional) pricing tier ($2.30/GB flat) to workspace-based resource using current (regional) pricing tier causes cost increase. Regional pricing varies by Log Analytics workspace location (e.g. WestUS $2.99/GB, CentralUS $2.76/GB).
solution: Review Azure Monitor Pricing docs to identify regions with lower ingestion costs. Associate the AI resource to a lower-cost region. Use AIMC tool (aimc.aisvc.visualstudio.com) Billing GA (v2) blade to verify previous pricing tier.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/75855

symptom: PageView duration field is always 0 in Application Insights for Single Page Applications (SPA) using the JavaScript SDK
rootCause: By design - the JavaScript SDK cannot accurately measure page load duration in SPAs because route changes do not trigger traditional page loads. The SDK defaults duration to 0 for SPA page views.
solution: Workarounds: (1) Manually calculate duration between route changes and pass to trackPageView(); (2) Use startTrackPageView()/stopTrackPageView() as timers; (3) For React apps, use applicationinsights-react-js example; (4) Set overridePageViewDuration config option.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/76137

symptom: Authorization error on the Application Insights blade when navigating to Web App or Function App in App Services - user cannot see whether an AI resource is connected
rootCause: The AI blade on App Services requires RBAC action 'microsoft.web/sites/config/list/actions' on microsoft.web/sites scope. Users with reader-like roles lack this permission. This is an App Services design choice, not an Azure Monitor issue.
solution: Assign a role with 'microsoft.web/sites/config/list/actions' (Website Contributor, Contributor, or Owner) at the microsoft.web/sites scope (NOT the AI resource scope). Transfer case SAP to App Services: Azure/Web App (Windows)/Configuration and Management/Creating or deleting resources.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/76573

symptom: Application Insights Breeze ingestion endpoints do not support IPv6 - customers with IPv6-only virtual networks cannot send telemetry
rootCause: By design - Application Insights ingestion endpoints currently only support IPv4. AMPLS also cannot be joined to IPv6-only networks.
solution: No workaround available. IPv6 ingestion support is on the product roadmap (rough ETA ~March 2025). Inform customers the ingestion team is actively working on IPv6 support.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/80194

symptom: Deployment fails in West Europe region with error 'The selected region is currently not accepting new customers' (aka.ms/locationineligible)
rootCause: ARM Region Access System Policy applied to West Europe due to power/capacity constraints. New customer tenants are blocked from deploying any resources in the region. Existing tenants with resources in West Europe are unaffected.
solution: Transfer case to SAP: Azure/Service and subscription limits (quotas)/Unable to access West Europe region. Customer communication: 'A tenant block was applied for West Europe as the region is not currently accepting new customers. We are transferring your request to our capacity team.'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/80417

symptom: Application Insights metric alerts show 'Other Values' aggregation for Cloud Role Names when there are more than 100 unique role names or role instances
rootCause: Application Insights Standard Metrics enforce a limit of 100 unique values per custom metric dimension. Values exceeding this limit are aggregated as 'Other Values'. With 282+ role names or 3000+ role instances, majority bucketed under 'Other Values'.
solution: Replace or supplement metric alerts with log-based alerts (using AppRequests or AppExceptions tables) which retain access to all unique dimension values without the 100-value limit.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/84385

symptom: Status Monitor V1 MSI download fails or scripts using go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=522371 stop working after March 31, 2025 retirement
rootCause: Status Monitor V1 is retired and the download share hosting the MSI has been removed. By design.
solution: Upgrade to Application Insights Agent (Status Monitor V2). Update any scripts/code that download the old MSI to point to the new agent: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/app/application-insights-asp-net-agent. Existing instrumented apps continue working; only the download location is removed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/88800

symptom: Creating Private Endpoint for Log Analytics workspace or Application Insights component fails with error 400: SubscriptionNotRegisteredForFeature Microsoft.Network/AllowPrivateEndpoints
rootCause: Users are trying to directly associate a Private Endpoint to an Azure Monitor resource (LA workspace or AI component) via ARM/Bicep/Terraform. This is not supported — PE must be connected through Azure Monitor Private Link Scope (AMPLS).
solution: Create an Azure Monitor Private Link Scope (AMPLS) resource and associate the PE with the AMPLS instead of directly with the LA workspace or AI component. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/private-link-configure
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/93264

symptom: Programmatic deployments (ARM/Bicep/DevOps/Terraform) of new Application Insights resources fail with internal error, HTTP 500, or job hangs on creation after April 1, 2025
rootCause: Blank or empty WorkspaceResourceId in the deployment template/script. Classic Application Insights resources were retired in April 2025; all new resources must be workspace-based.
solution: Populate WorkspaceResourceId with a valid Log Analytics workspace resource ID in the deployment template. Alternatively, ensure Azure Policy is configured with proper tags for resource group creation. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/app/managed-workspaces
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/94055

symptom: Users receive Azure alert or email notification: 'Update your remote endpoints TLS configurations for Azure Monitor Availability Tests'
rootCause: By design. Microsoft is deprecating TLS 1.0, 1.1, and legacy 1.2 cipher suites and elliptical curves. Applications monitored by Availability Tests must update their TLS configuration.
solution: Direct customers to the administrator of their application or server hosting the monitored application to update TLS configuration. Point to the TLS deprecation reference material on the wiki for detailed guidance.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/94124

symptom: Application Insights autoinstrumentation for .NET Core fails silently — missing telemetry in portal. Memory dump shows ASPNETCORE_HOSTINGSTARTUPASSEMBLIES does not contain Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.StartupBootstrapper
rootCause: Customer modified hosting startup assembly settings (e.g. set ASPNETCORE_HOSTINGSTARTUPASSEMBLIES to custom assemblies only), overriding the StartupBootstrapper DLL required for autoinstrumentation to inject the SDK.
solution: Ensure ASPNETCORE_HOSTINGSTARTUPASSEMBLIES includes 'Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.StartupBootstrapper'. Add it via UseSetting(WebHostDefaults.HostingStartupAssembliesKey, "Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.StartupBootstrapper;OtherAssembly;..."). Diagnose with: 1) memory dump !peb to check env vars, 2) WebAppsAppInsightsExtension Kusto table to check autoinstrumentation logs for loop-like events.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/95434

symptom: Understanding codeless/auto-instrumentation architecture for .NET and .NET Core — Status Monitor V2, Application Insights Extension, Azure VM extension, on-premises agent
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-Code-less-Explained-for-Net-and-Net-Core.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Concepts/Code-less Explained for .Net and .Net Core

symptom: PowerShell cmdlets reference for Application Insights Agent (Status Monitor V2): Enable-ApplicationInsightsMonitoring, Disable, Get config/status for on-premises IIS server instrumentation
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-Cmdlets-and-API-reference-for-Application-Insights-Agent.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/ASP.Net on-premises agent/Cmdlets and API reference exploration for the Application Insights Agent

symptom: Get-ApplicationInsightsMonitoringStatus cmdlet throws error "GetOutOfProcInfo: The object identifier does not represent a valid object (HRESULT: 0x800710D8)" and NullReferenceException when running on IIS server
rootCause: Two possible causes: (1) FTP site configured in IIS causes the cmdlet to fail when parsing applicationHost.config bindings with protocol=ftp - this is a known bug in the cmdlet (maintenance mode, will not fix); (2) Malformed applicationHost.config with applicationPool referencing a non-existent pool
solution: For FTP site: remove the FTP site from IIS before running the cmdlet, or swap applicationHost.config with a version without FTP sites. For malformed config: fix the applicationHost.config XML. Note: the cmdlet failure does NOT impact App Insights agent functionality - telemetry attach and ingestion continue to work normally.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/72419

symptom: Cloud/App Role Names (AppRoleName/cloud_RoleName) randomly disappear in Application Insights Performance or Failures blades when time range exceeds 1 hour and extends beyond 24 hours, despite data being accessible in shorter time ranges
rootCause: Application Insights Standard Metrics enforces a limit of 100 unique dimensions. When a component has more than 100 unique cloud/app role names, metrics accuracy degrades and role names may disappear from the UI.
solution: Reduce unique cloud/app role names to fewer than 100 per Application Insights component by distributing or offboarding applications to separate components. Use KQL queries (requests/exceptions tables with cloud_RoleName or AppRoleName) to audit the count of unique role names over 7 days.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/73189

symptom: Application Insights Profiler generates exceptions: COMException "Exception from HRESULT: 0xE111005E" after StartProfiler, and InvalidOperationException "There is no active profiling session" after StopProfiler
rootCause: Antares platform version 102 changed concurrent profiling session support - only one profiling session per App Service Plan is allowed. Attempting another session on a different app within the same plan triggers these exceptions.
solution: Known issue - Profiler agent update scheduled to handle these exceptions internally (ETA early 2025, rollout via Antares rapid update ~4 weeks). No performance impact on the application. Customer-ready: profiling session was deferred because another session is already in progress on the same App Service Plan.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/73978

symptom: Cloud Role Instance (cloud_RoleInstance) mismatch between App Service and Application Insights on Linux-based App Service - App Insights uses WEBSITE_INSTANCE_ID instead of the Host Name shown in App Service detector
rootCause: By design - Application Insights .NET SDK has always used WEBSITE_INSTANCE_ID as role instance ID for Azure App Service. This is standardized across the Azure Monitor Exporter for all languages. No modification planned as it would be a breaking change.
solution: Implement a Telemetry Initializer that uses the ComputerName environment variable, which properly exposes the Instance ID matching the Host Name emitted by Linux App Service.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/75701

symptom: Higher data ingestion billing after migrating from classic Application Insights resource to workspace-based resource
rootCause: Migration from basic (non-regional) pricing tier at $2.30/GB to regional pricing tier where costs depend on Log Analytics workspace region (e.g., WestUS $2.99/GB, CentralUS $2.76/GB). The non-regional pricing billed flat rate regardless of region, but workspace-based resources use regional pricing.
solution: Review Azure Monitor Pricing documentation to identify regions with lower per-GB costs. Associate the Application Insights resource with a Log Analytics workspace in a lower-cost region. Use AIMC tool (https://aimc.aisvc.visualstudio.com/Manage/Customers) to verify if the component was previously on basic pricing tier.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/75855

symptom: PageView telemetry duration field always shows 0 for Single Page Applications (SPA) using Application Insights JavaScript SDK
rootCause: By design - SPA route changes do not trigger traditional page loads, so the default PageView duration measurement does not apply. Documented in GitHub issue #1139.
solution: Multiple workarounds: (1) Manually calculate duration between route changes and pass to trackPageView(); (2) Use startTrackPageView()/stopTrackPageView() as timers; (3) Use applicationinsights-react-js example for measuring SPA page duration; (4) Set overridePageViewDuration configuration option.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/76137

symptom: Authorization error when clicking Application Insights blade on web app or function app in App Services portal - users cannot see UI or determine if App Insights is connected
rootCause: Missing RBAC permission for action microsoft.web/sites/config/list/actions on the web/function app scope. Reader-like roles lack this permission. This is an App Services team design choice, not an Azure Monitoring issue.
solution: Assign a role that allows microsoft.web/sites/config/list/actions (Website Contributor, Contributor, or Owner) scoped to microsoft.web/sites (NOT to the App Insights resource). Common misstep: assigning roles at App Insights scope does not help because the enforcing RP is Microsoft.Web. Transfer case to App Services SAP.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/76573

symptom: Application Insights Breeze Ingestion endpoints do not support IPv6 - customers with IPv6-only virtual networks cannot send telemetry data
rootCause: By design - Application Insights Ingestion endpoints currently only support IPv4. AMPLS also cannot join IPv6-only networks.
solution: No workaround available. IPv6 ingestion support is on the roadmap (rough estimate March 2025). Inform customers that the ingestion product team is actively working on IPv6 support.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/80194

symptom: Deployment fails in West Europe region with error "The selected region is currently not accepting new customers" - affects all Azure products and services in the region
rootCause: ARM Region Access System Policy applied at AAD tenant level to manage long-term capacity constraint in West Europe due to unavailability of additional power. Existing tenants with resources can continue; new tenants or tenants without WE resources are blocked.
solution: Transfer case to capacity team SAP: Azure/Service and subscription limits (quotas)/Unable to access West Europe region. Customer communication: A tenant block was applied for West Europe as the region is not currently accepting new customers. This is NOT an Application Insights issue.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/80417

symptom: Cloud Role Names aggregating as "Other Values" in Application Insights metric alerts - customer has 282 role names and 3000+ role instances exceeding the 100 unique dimension limit
rootCause: Application Insights Standard Metrics limits custom metric dimensions to 100 unique values. Values beyond the limit are aggregated as Other Values.
solution: Replace or supplement metric alerts with log-based alerts, which have access to all unique values without the 100 dimension limit. By design.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/84385

symptom: After installing Az.ApplicationMonitor module, running Enable-ApplicationInsightsMonitoring fails with import error
rootCause: The PowerShell session still has the old module context cached; the newly installed module is not imported
solution: Close the existing PowerShell session and start a new Administrator session, then run Import-Module -Name Az.ApplicationMonitor before Enable-ApplicationInsightsMonitoring
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/ASP.Net on-premises agent/Enabling configuration for the Application Insights Agent

symptom: Application Insights Agent (Status Monitor v2) fails to instrument applications running as IIS nested applications
rootCause: The Application Insights Agent does not support IIS nested application configurations where apps are nested within another application in the IIS hierarchy
solution: Restructure IIS sites to avoid nested application layout. Each monitored application should be a top-level site or application, not nested under another app. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/troubleshoot/azure/azure-monitor/app-insights/agent/status-monitor-v2-troubleshoot#iis-nested-applications
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/ASP.Net on-premises agent/Requirements for the Application Insights Agent

symptom: Application Insights Agent fails or conflicts when IIS Shared Configuration is enabled on the server
rootCause: The AI Agent modifies applicationHost.config during enablement, which conflicts with IIS Shared Configuration where config files are stored centrally and shared across servers
solution: Disable IIS Shared Configuration before enabling the AI Agent, or follow the workaround at: https://learn.microsoft.com/troubleshoot/azure/azure-monitor/app-insights/agent/status-monitor-v2-troubleshoot#conflict-with-iis-shared-configuration. Note: IIS Shared Configuration was introduced in IIS 7; older versions do not have this concern
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/ASP.Net on-premises agent/Requirements for the Application Insights Agent

symptom: .NET Core App Service autoinstrumentation shows 'backed off' or 'failed' status in Kudu (/applicationinsights endpoint) or AppLens Auto Instrumentation detector; telemetry not collected via codeless attach
rootCause: The Application Insights SDK was already manually added/loaded in the application code. When autoinstrumentation detects an existing SDK, it backs off to avoid duplicate telemetry collection
solution: Option 1: Remove the manual Application Insights SDK from the application code and rely solely on autoinstrumentation. Option 2: In Azure Portal > App Service > Monitoring > Application Insights > Instrument your application > enable 'Interop with Application Insights SDK' (.NET Core only) to override the manual SDK. Validate fix via Kudu Advanced Tools (append /applicationinsights to URL) or AppLens Auto Instrumentation detector
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/App Service Web app .Net Core/Enabling Application Insights

symptom: Cannot enable Application Insights autoinstrumentation for .NET Core App Service web app; the option is not available or not supported
rootCause: The .NET Core application is built on a non-LTS (Long Term Support) version. Autoinstrumentation only supports LTS versions of .NET Core
solution: Upgrade the application to a supported .NET Core LTS version. Check https://dotnet.microsoft.com/platform/support/policy/dotnet-core for current LTS versions. Note: autoinstrumentation currently uses classic AI SDK but will transition to OpenTelemetry in the future
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/App Service Web app .Net Core/Requirements for autoinstrumention of .Net Core web apps

solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-java-self-diagnostics-logs.md

symptom: Application Insights Agent on IIS fails to monitor applications; agent does not collect telemetry
rootCause: IIS nested applications are present. The Application Insights Agent does not support IIS nested app configurations where a folder is made into a sub-application within a parent site
solution: Remove IIS nested applications or restructure them as separate sites. Verify in IIS Manager under Sites for nested layouts. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/troubleshoot/azure/azure-monitor/app-insights/agent/status-monitor-v2-troubleshoot#iis-nested-applications
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/ASP.Net on-premises agent/Requirements for the Application Insights Agent

symptom: Application Insights Agent on IIS fails or behaves unexpectedly during installation
rootCause: IIS shared configuration is enabled, which conflicts with the Application Insights Agent modifications to applicationHost.config
solution: Disable IIS shared configuration before installing the AI Agent, or apply the workaround: https://learn.microsoft.com/troubleshoot/azure/azure-monitor/app-insights/agent/status-monitor-v2-troubleshoot#conflict-with-iis-shared-configuration
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/ASP.Net on-premises agent/Requirements for the Application Insights Agent

symptom: App Service .NET Core autoinstrumentation status shows backed off or failed attach; Application Insights SDK binaries did not load
rootCause: An existing manually instrumented (classic) Application Insights SDK in the application conflicts with codeless autoinstrumentation, causing it to back off
solution: Enable the Interop with Application Insights SDK option in Application Insights configuration on App Service (Monitoring > Application Insights > Instrument your application > .NET Core). This overrides the existing classic SDK. Alternatively remove the manual SDK NuGet packages

symptom: Kudu /applicationinsights status page shows no application running or no instrumentation info displayed
rootCause: The ASP.NET Core application has not been deployed to the App Service web app yet, or the application process is not currently running
solution: Ensure the application is deployed to the App Service (e.g. via Visual Studio publish) and verify the app is running before checking the Kudu /applicationinsights status page. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/troubleshoot/azure/azure-monitor/app-insights/telemetry/auto-instrumentation-troubleshoot

symptom: Java App Service autoinstrumentation producing unexpected behavior, duplicate agent loading, or application startup failures after enabling Application Insights
rootCause: User manually added applicationinsights-agent-#.#.#.jar reference in JAVA_OPTS or CATALINA_OPTS environment variables, conflicting with App Service auto-instrumentation which manages its own agent injection automatically
solution: In App Service > Settings > Environment Variables, check JAVA_OPTS and CATALINA_OPTS values; remove any reference to applicationinsights-agent jar file. Autoinstrumentation manages agent injection automatically and manual references cause conflicts.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/App Service Web app Java/Enabling autoinstrumentation for a Java web application

symptom: Python App Service autoinstrumentation is not available or fails to enable on Windows operating system
rootCause: Python autoinstrumentation for App Service is only supported on Linux; Windows is not currently supported (as of Oct 2024)
solution: Use Linux App Service plan for Python autoinstrumentation. For Windows Python apps, use manual instrumentation with OpenTelemetry SDK instead.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/App Service Web app Python/Requirements for autoinstrumention of Python web apps

symptom: Python App Service autoinstrumentation not working for container-based (Docker) deployment
rootCause: Python autoinstrumentation only supports Code publish type; Container publish type is not currently supported (as of Oct 2024)
solution: For containerized Python apps, use manual instrumentation with OpenTelemetry SDK. Switch to Code publish type if autoinstrumentation is required.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/App Service Web app Python/Requirements for autoinstrumention of Python web apps

symptom: Duplicate telemetry data appearing in Application Insights for Python App Service application
rootCause: Both autoinstrumentation (codeless attach) and manual SDK instrumentation are enabled simultaneously, causing telemetry to be collected and sent twice
solution: Use only one instrumentation method. Disable autoinstrumentation if manual SDK is in use (Portal > App Service > Monitoring > Application Insights > Disable), or remove manual SDK references (opencensus/opentelemetry packages) from requirements.txt if using autoinstrumentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/App Service Web app Python/Requirements for autoinstrumention of Python web apps

symptom: AppLens App Insights Feature Status detector shows Profiler and Snapshot Debugger as enabled for Python App Service, but these features do not actually function
rootCause: Known false positive in AppLens detector - Profiler and Snapshot Debugger are not supported for Python applications; the detector incorrectly reports them as enabled
solution: Ignore Profiler and Snapshot Debugger status indicators in AppLens for Python apps. These features are only available for .NET applications. Do not troubleshoot Profiler/Snapshot issues for Python workloads.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/App Service Web app Python/Validating successful autoinstrumentation with AppLens

symptom: AppLens shows autoinstrumentation succeeded (binaries injected successfully) but no telemetry data appears in Application Insights component
rootCause: Network connectivity issues preventing telemetry from reaching the Application Insights ingestion endpoint (firewalls, NSGs, proxy configurations, or Private Link misconfiguration blocking outbound traffic)
solution: 1) Verify autoinstrumentation status via AppLens detector shows success. 2) Check ASC Ingestion tab to confirm if telemetry is being received. 3) Verify network connectivity to ingestion endpoints. 4) Check NSG rules, firewall, proxy settings, AMPLS configuration. 5) Use KQL: union * | where timestamp >= ago(24h) | project timestamp, sdkVersion, itemType, cloud_RoleName to verify data flow.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/App Service Web app Java/Validating successful autoinstrumentation with AppLens
