symptom: App Insights support boundary for Azure Cosmos DB - dependency tracking questions or issues with Cosmos DB calls not appearing in Application Insights dependency telemetry
rootCause: Prior to June 2023, Cosmos DB was only trackable as HTTP dependency. After June 2023 update, native Cosmos DB protocol is automatically hooked and tracked by Application Insights (similar to SQL/TDS protocol).
solution: For Cosmos DB dependency tracking in App Insights: post-June 2023, native protocol is auto-tracked. For Cosmos DB-side issues, route to queue: MSaaS Big Data - Database, SAP: Azure Cosmos DB, Area Path: Azure/Cosmos DB. PG email: azurecosmossdk@microsoft.com, IcM: Azure Cosmos DB/SQL API SDKs and Spark Connector. Collect: precise ask, Cosmos DB resource URI, App Insights resource URI.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Azure CosmosDB

symptom: App Insights support boundary for Azure Dashboards - dashboard rendering issues or incorrect data display involving Application Insights metrics in Azure portal dashboards
solution: Distinguish between: (1) Functionality/rendering issues with dashboard itself -> Azure Portal Team, SAP: Azure/Portal/Issues with Dashboard Loading or Refreshing. (2) Data correctness issues where App Insights data is incorrect -> Azure Monitor owns it. Collect: precise ask, resource URIs, App Insights resource URI.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Azure Dashboards

symptom: App Insights support boundary for Azure Databricks - customer references stale MSDN article about monitoring Databricks jobs with Application Insights using unsupported community Python SDK (applicationinsights 0.11.5 on PyPI)
rootCause: The public article (MSDN Magazine June 2018) is stale and instructs users to use an unsupported community Application Insights Python SDK (pypi.org/project/applicationinsights/0.11.5). This SDK is not maintained or supported by Microsoft.
solution: If customer is configuring the community Python SDK referenced in the stale article, engage Azure Databricks support team to work directly with Databricks PG. Do NOT troubleshoot the community SDK. SAP: Azure/Databricks/Monitoring and diagnostics/Diagnostics logs. Collect: precise ask, Databricks resource URI, App Insights resource URI.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Azure Databricks

symptom: App Insights support boundary for Azure Logic Apps Standard - Application Insights configuration or data collection issues in Logic Apps Standard workflows, need to find host.json for telemetry config
rootCause: Logic Apps Standard runs on Azure Functions backend, so App Insights integration follows Azure Functions SDK implementation. This is NOT an integration scenario owned by Azure Monitor. Configuration is managed through host.json like standard Azure Functions.
solution: Logic Apps Standard uses Function App backend -> App Insights config via host.json file. To access: Logic Apps resource -> Advanced Tools blade -> Go -> navigate to C:\home\site\wwwroot\host.json. For integration issues, route to Azure Functions team. SAP: Azure/Logic App-Logic App (Standard)/Tools/Application Insights. Collect: Function App resource URI, workflow names, App Insights resource URI.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Azure Logic Apps Standard

symptom: App Insights support boundary for Azure Machine Learning - monitoring integration questions or issues with ML services Application Insights enablement
solution: Machine Learning team provides prescribed means of enabling App Insights within ML services (see docs.microsoft.com/azure/machine-learning/how-to-enable-app-insights). For ML-specific monitoring issues, route to: SAP: Azure/Machine Learning/Monitoring/Other issue with model monitoring. Collect: precise ask, ML resource URIs, App Insights resource URI.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Azure Machine learning

symptom: App Insights support boundary for Azure Monitor Networks Insights - case misfiled under Application Insights for Network Insights or Load Balancer Insights which are NOT Application Insights features
rootCause: The Insights keyword causes confusion - Network Insights (including Load Balancer Insights) is a custom solution created by Azure Networking team. It is NOT related to Application Insights despite sharing the Insights naming.
solution: Network Insights / Load Balancer Insights -> owned by Azure Networking team, not Application Insights. If error is specifically about saving/editing the workbook displayed by Network Insights -> Azure Monitor workbook team can assist. SAP: Azure/Network Watcher/Network Insights/Topology view.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Azure Monitor Networks Insights

symptom: App Insights support boundary for Azure Service Fabric microservice level - ServiceFabric telemetry initializer NuGet package (SDK prefix serviceremoting) is deprecated and no longer actively maintained by PG
rootCause: The NuGet package Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.ServiceFabric is from an old collaboration between .NET and Service Fabric Product teams and is no longer active/maintained. No further updates or fixes will be made. OpenTelemetry distro replacement is under early discussion.
solution: CSS offers best-effort support only on this SDK. Telemetry from this package shows SDK prefix serviceremoting. The standard App Insights SDK can be added to microservices on Service Fabric containers same as standard web apps. For customer feedback/feature requests, direct to Azure Monitor feedback channel (feedback.azure.com). No specific SAP routing listed - handle as standard App Insights instrumentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Azure Service Fabric microservice level

symptom: App Insights support boundary for Azure Spring Apps (formerly Azure Spring Cloud) - monitoring integration and Application Insights enablement support routing
solution: Spring Apps provides prescribed means of enabling App Insights (see docs.microsoft.com/azure/spring-cloud/how-to-application-insights). For Spring Apps-specific integration issues, route to: SAP: Azure/Azure Spring Apps/Application Insights. Collect: precise ask, Spring Apps resource URIs, App Insights resource URI.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Azure Spring Apps (formerly known as Azure Spring Cloud service)

symptom: App Insights support boundary for Azure Static Web Apps - auto-instrumentation monitors backend APIs only (Functions, APIM, App Services, Container Apps), NOT the Static Web App resource itself
rootCause: Static Web Apps integration with Application Insights through auto-instrumentation only covers backend API endpoints (Azure Functions, API Management, App Services, Container Apps), not the Static Web App resource itself. The static content hosting is not monitored by App Insights.
solution: Supported by Azure Functions team. Distinguish: backend API monitoring (App Insights scope) vs static content hosting issues (not App Insights scope). SAP: Azure/Static Web Apps/Monitoring. Additional refs: AzureAppService wiki - Static Web Apps Monitoring page. Collect: Static web app resource URI, specific API names, App Insights resource URI.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Azure Static Web Apps

symptom: Blazor application telemetry incomplete or correlation broken in Application Insights - Blazor (client and server side) is officially unsupported by the Application Insights SDK team
rootCause: The Application Insights SDK team never built in support for Blazor apps (neither client-side nor server-side). Telemetry correlation, missing telemetry types, and incomplete experience are expected in unsupported Blazor environments.
solution: Advise customer that Blazor is unsupported for Application Insights. For custom telemetry needs, use direct API calls (TelemetryClient / TrackEvent / TrackDependency). See: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/api-custom-events-metrics
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Blazor Applications

symptom: Dynamics 365 (BC/CE/FO/Commerce/PowerApps/PowerPlatform) telemetry issues with Application Insights - support boundary and escalation routing guide
solution: See routing guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-dynamics-365-support-boundaries.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Dynamics 365 (BC, CE, FO, Commerce, PowerApps, PowerPlatform)

symptom: Internal Microsoft team opens support case about Geneva Monitoring telemetry - Geneva is a separate internal-only service, not Application Insights
rootCause: Geneva Monitoring is an internal Microsoft service separate from Application Insights. Geneva has its own classic SDK and OpenTelemetry options. Application Insights sends to public ingestion endpoints, not Geneva.
solution: Redirect internal customer to Geneva support: https://eng.ms/docs/products/geneva/support/supportpolicy. If the internal team uses standard AI SDKs/OpenTelemetry to send to an AI resource (not Geneva), then AI CSS can assist.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Geneva

symptom: LDAP dependency calls not appearing in Application Insights dependency tracking - LDAP is not automatically tracked by Application Insights SDK
rootCause: LDAP calls are not in the list of automatically tracked dependencies for Application Insights. The SDK does not auto-detect LDAP protocol calls.
solution: Use manual dependency tracking via TrackDependency API: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/asp-net-dependencies#manually-tracking-dependencies. For Active Directory LDAP issues, collab/transfer to: Windows Servers/Active Directory/LDAP. For third-party directory services, redirect to vendor.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/LDAP

symptom: .NET Orleans application telemetry issues with Application Insights or OpenTelemetry integration - Orleans telemetry packages not supported by Azure Monitor team
rootCause: .NET Orleans integrates with AI/OTel via its own NuGet packages (Microsoft.Orleans.OrleansTelemetryConsumers.AI for 3.x, OpenTelemetry exporters for 7.0). These packages are not owned or supported by the Azure Monitoring team.
solution: Redirect to Orleans team. SAP: Developer Tools/ASP.NET Core/Orleans. Orleans 7.0 uses OTel exporters (Prometheus); Orleans 3.x uses OrleansTelemetryConsumers.AI NuGet. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/orleans/host/monitoring/
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/NET Orleans

symptom: Issue with Azure Monitor Query client library (azure-monitor-query SDK for Java/.NET) confused with Application Insights Java Agent - Azure SDK is a separate team
rootCause: Azure SDK for Java/dotnet (azure-monitor-query libraries) is owned by the Azure SDK team, NOT the Application Insights team. Must not confuse Azure SDK for Java with the Application Insights Java Agent.
solution: Collaborate with Azure SDK developer support per: https://supportability.visualstudio.com/AzureMonitor/_wiki/wikis/AzureMonitor.wiki/616302/Collaborating-with-Azure-SDK-developer-support. The AI Java Agent (java-in-process-agent) IS supported by Azure Monitoring team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Open Source Azure SDKs

symptom: Application Insights Profiler support boundary - which profiler scenarios are supported vs unsupported by Azure Monitoring CSS
solution: See support boundary guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-profiler-support-boundaries.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Profiler

symptom: SharePoint Application Insights JavaScript SDK injection not working or deprecated - legacy injection no longer supported, SPFx extension required
rootCause: Legacy SharePoint JavaScript SDK injection (per previous docs) is no longer supported due to security concerns. The old method of injecting JS SDK into SharePoint pages is deprecated.
solution: Customer must use SharePoint Framework (SPFx) custom extension to install Application Insights on SharePoint sites. AI CSS supports only the JS SDK data collection questions; SPFx extension deployment issues go to SharePoint team (SAP: SharePoint/SharePoint Development/SharePoint Framework (SPFx)).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/SharePoint legacy and SPFx

symptom: Application Insights auto-instrumentation not working on SiteCore application - SiteCore assemblies conflict with AI auto-instrumentation attachment to w3wp process
rootCause: SiteCore solutions come pre-integrated with their own Application Insights assemblies. These SiteCore-introduced assemblies can directly affect the ability of AI auto-instrumentation (App Services/AI Agent/VM extension) to attach to the w3wp process and collect telemetry.
solution: AI CSS should validate backend ingestion (daily cap, network connectivity, etc.) is working. If issue is client-side where auto-instrumentation fails due to SiteCore assemblies, redirect customer to SiteCore support for correct telemetry configuration guidance.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/SiteCore

symptom: Visual Studio App Center telemetry not appearing in Application Insights - App Center uses custom export service (not SDK) with User-Agent "AppCenter.Export"
rootCause: App Center collects telemetry from mobile apps and sends it to AI via a custom REST client (App Center Export service), NOT the Application Insights SDK. The export service uses User-Agent "AppCenter.Export". Export issues are on the App Center side.
solution: For export issues, collaborate with App Center team. SAP: Developer Tools/Visual Studio App Center/App Center/Analytics/Application Insights. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/appcenter/analytics/export
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Visual Studio App Center

symptom: Custom telemetry (trackTrace, trackEvent, trackDependency) not appearing in Application Insights logs despite SDK integration and manual API calls
rootCause: SDK onboarding incomplete or basic auto-collected telemetry not working. Custom calls cannot succeed if foundation telemetry pipeline is broken. Common causes: wrong connection string, missing SDK packages, TelemetryClient not properly initialized.
solution: 1) Validate basic auto-collected telemetry (requests, dependencies) is flowing first. 2) Ensure latest SDK version per sdk-support-guidance. 3) Start simple: test trackTrace() call and verify it appears in Traces table. 4) Only then add complex custom telemetry. 5) If causing exceptions, reproduce in isolated sample app to narrow scope.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights setup and customization/Create custom telemetry

symptom: Application Insights telemetry metrics (request/exception counts) not showing in Visual Studio method annotations after adding Application Insights as connected service
rootCause: Missing or incomplete configuration in .csproj file. Both ApplicationInsightsResourceId and ApplicationInsightsAnnotationResourceId must be present in PropertyGroup for method annotations to work.
solution: In Solution Explorer, click the project name to open .csproj. Add both properties inside <PropertyGroup>: <ApplicationInsightsResourceId>/subscriptions/{sub}/resourcegroups/{rg}/providers/microsoft.insights/components/{name}</ApplicationInsightsResourceId> and <ApplicationInsightsAnnotationResourceId> with same value.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights setup and customization/IDE setup (Visual Studio, Maven)

symptom: Cannot send auto-collected telemetry to multiple Application Insights resources from a single application; customer wants to duplicate telemetry across multiple AI components
rootCause: Design limitation across all Application Insights SDKs (.NET, Java, Node.js, Python, JavaScript). Auto-collected telemetry can only be sent to a single Application Insights resource. This is consistent behavior, not a bug.
solution: Only manually created telemetry (via TelemetryClient or OpenTelemetry API) can target a secondary Application Insights resource. Auto-collected telemetry remains bound to single resource. For environment separation (dev/staging/prod), use separate AI resources with connection string switching. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/separate-resources
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights setup and customization/Multiple Application Insights resources

symptom: Not receiving email notifications from Application Insights Availability Tests alerts despite test failures being detected
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: 1) Alert rule conditions not actually met (threshold/location count mismatch). 2) Alert fired but notification blocked by corporate email systems that reject group/distribution list aliases. 3) Alert processing rules suppressing notifications.
solution: 1) Validate alert conditions: check if the alert actually fired using Alert History in portal. 2) Verify alert rule evaluation conditions match expected behavior. 3) If alert fired but no email: test with individual email address instead of group alias. 4) Check Action Group notification history. 5) See TSG: /Alerts/Troubleshooting-Guides/Troubleshooting-email-notifications-not-being-received
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Availability Tests/Alerts and notifications

symptom: Customer needs guidance on migrating from classic Application Insights SDK to OpenTelemetry distro: benefits, feature parity, and per-language migration path
solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-classic-sdk-to-otel-migration.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights setup and customization/Classic SDK to OpenTelemetry migration

symptom: Customer needs to filter, modify, or customize auto-collected telemetry in Application Insights using telemetry processors, initializers, or DCR ingestion-time transformations
solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-filter-modify-telemetry.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights setup and customization/Filter or modify collected telemetry

symptom: Customer needs getting-started guidance for Application Insights: what it is, how to enable/configure, autoinstrumentation vs manual instrumentation, classic SDK vs OpenTelemetry
solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-getting-started.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights setup and customization/Getting started

symptom: Application Insights telemetry blocked by proxy or firewall: need to whitelist endpoints, IPs, and ports for SDK ingestion, Live Metrics, availability tests, and Status Monitor
solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-telemetry-proxy-firewall.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights setup and customization/Telemetry through proxy or firewall

symptom: Customer asks for help with Visual Studio Snapshot Debugger feature (not Application Insights Snapshot Debugger) - unable to generate or view snapshots using VS IDE debugger
rootCause: Visual Studio Snapshot Debugger is owned by the Visual Studio team, not Azure Monitor. This feature offers similar functionality to the Application Insights Snapshot Debugger but is a separate product.
solution: Route to Visual Studio team via SAP: Developer Tools/Visual Studio 2022/Visual Studio Enterprise 2022/Debuggers and Analyzers/Visual Studio Debugger. Contact: DLsnaphelp@microsoft.com
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Visual Studio Snapshot Debugger

symptom: End-to-end transaction view in Application Insights is missing telemetry or appears truncated for large operations - not all telemetry items shown in Gantt chart
rootCause: E2E view enforces two limits: max 10,000 telemetry items per operation_Id and max 8-hour time window. Linked operations (_ms.Linked) are not counted but can cause memory/performance issues or browser crashes.
solution: Use Logs view to query full operation data. Verify limits with KQL: union * | where operation_Id == id | count and summarize min/max timestamp. For linked operations exceeding 10K, rely on Logs view exclusively.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights portal experiences/End-to-end Transaction Details and search

symptom: End-to-end Transaction Details shows 'More details for this call are not available' with 'Telemetry could not be found' when clicking a dependency in the Gantt chart
rootCause: Backend request telemetry with matching parent ID not found. Causes: (1) sampled out, (2) delayed during ingestion, (3) filtered by custom TelemetryInitializer, (4) filtered by Data Collection Rule (DCR).
solution: Search for backend request record with matching parent ID. Check if telemetry was sampled, delayed, or filtered. Re-run operation in E2E to capture all telemetry. Investigate SDK sampling, TelemetryInitializer filters, and DCR rules.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights portal experiences/End-to-end Transaction Details and search

symptom: Failures or Performance blade shows metric-based data (charts, counts) but log-based sections show 'No available results' - partial telemetry displayed
rootCause: Log Analytics workspace reached configured daily cap. Metrics and logs have different ingestion processes - daily cap blocks log ingestion but metrics continue, leading to partial blade experience.
solution: Check if underlying Log Analytics workspace daily cap was reached. Metric ingestion is separate from log ingestion. When daily cap triggers, metric-based visuals work but log-based data (Operation Names, drill-down) becomes unavailable until cap resets.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights portal experiences/Failures investigation

symptom: Live Metrics shows 'Not Available: could not connect to your application' demo animation - no SDK connection to QuickPulse service
rootCause: Multiple causes: app not running, SDK/language not supporting Live Metrics, missing all telemetry, browser ad blockers, TLS < 1.2, .NET Framework missing config, network blocking Live Metrics endpoints.
solution: Diagnostic steps: (1) Confirm app running, (2) Check SDK support per docs, (3) Verify ingestion via ASC, (4) Disable browser extensions, (5) Ensure TLS 1.2 outgoing (App Service TLS/SSL only affects incoming), (6) .NET Framework: validate config, (7) Test Live Metrics endpoint connectivity (different from ingestion endpoints).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights portal experiences/Live Metrics

symptom: Live Metrics shows instance ID instead of friendly server name for Linux hosted App Service or Azure Functions on Linux
rootCause: By design, Linux App Services set cloud_RoleInstance to container instance ID instead of friendly name. Azure Functions on Linux sets instanceId as cloud_RoleInstance, differing from Portal Settings > Instances.
solution: This is by design for Linux. Validate instance ID using AppLens Instance Health detector - modify query to include RoleInstanceId in LinuxRoleInstanceHeartBeats summarize and final project. Normal for Live Metrics server count to be lower than allocated instances.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights portal experiences/Live Metrics

symptom: Live Metrics shows '<Empty Role>' as Role Name for on-premises apps. For Node.js, Role Name in Live Metrics does not match collected telemetry role name.
rootCause: On-premises: no cloud role context exists, defaults to '<Empty Role>'. Node.js: known bug (fixed v2.6.0) where Live Metrics Role Name mismatches collected telemetry.
solution: For .NET on-premises: create custom ITelemetryInitializer with parameterless constructor setting cloud.RoleName. Must register in ApplicationInsights.config (not just code) for QuickPulseTelemetryModule. For Node.js: upgrade to v2.6.0+.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights portal experiences/Live Metrics

symptom: Application Insights connection string does not contain LiveEndpoint parameter - customer asks why Live Metrics endpoint is missing
rootCause: QuickPulse (Live Metrics backend) not deployed to every region. If not available in resource region, LiveEndpoint is omitted from connection string.
solution: Expected behavior. SDKs default to global Live Metrics endpoint (rt.services.visualstudio.com) when LiveEndpoint absent. No action needed unless network restrictions block global endpoint.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights portal experiences/Live Metrics

symptom: Live Metrics shows Dependency and Failure data but CPU and Committed Memory metrics are not displayed - on-premises with SDK/Agent
rootCause: CPU and Committed Memory come from Performance Counters requiring proper identity/permissions. Dependency/Failure data collected differently, independent of Performance Counter access.
solution: Check permissions for the identity used by instrumented process to read Performance Counters. Refer to System performance counters Prerequisites documentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights portal experiences/Live Metrics

symptom: Kusto query in Logs blade fails with 'Failed to resolve table or column or scalar expression named' error (e.g. requests table not found)
rootCause: Query uses table names for wrong scope. App Insights classic: non-prefixed (requests, dependencies). Log Analytics workspace: requires App prefix (AppRequests, AppDependencies). May be wrong blade/scope.
solution: Use App-prefixed table names in Log Analytics scope, non-prefixed in App Insights scope. Verify correct blade/scope. Check Public Network Access and workspace Access Control Mode setting.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights portal experiences/Logs investigation (Kusto queries)

symptom: Kusto query results in Azure portal Logs blade appear truncated or incomplete - not all expected rows returned
rootCause: Portal truncates results at 30,000 rows (service limit). Notification banner appears when truncation occurs.
solution: Use Log Analytics REST API with batching for large datasets. Note: query investigations limited to queries less than 30 days old (data plane API logs retention).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights portal experiences/Logs investigation (Kusto queries)

symptom: In Metrics Explorer, Apply Splitting button is disabled (greyed out) when charting a custom metric in Application Insights
rootCause: Custom metric has no dimensions, or App Insights not configured to send custom metrics with dimensions to Azure Metric Store.
solution: Go to App Insights > Usage and estimated costs > Custom Metrics (Preview) > Set 'Send custom metrics to Azure Metric Store' to 'With Dimensions'. Enables dimension-based splitting and alerting.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights portal experiences/Metrics investigation

symptom: Metrics blade chart only displays last 30 days of data even though customMetrics/AppMetrics log data retained for longer (90 days default)
rootCause: Platform/custom metrics have specific pre-aggregated retention period differing from log-based retention. Metrics blade shows data from metric store, not logs.
solution: Expected behavior per Azure Monitor metrics retention. For older data, use log-based queries (customMetrics/AppMetrics tables) or Log-based namespace in Metrics Explorer.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights portal experiences/Metrics investigation

symptom: Application Map missing details, empty, showing incorrect labels, or not loading - multi-step TSG for Application Map portal experience
solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-Application-Map-TSG.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights portal experiences/Application Map

symptom: Performance blade shows unexpected results, incorrect durations, missing operations, or Error retrieving data - multi-step TSG for Performance Investigation
solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-Performance-Investigation-TSG.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights portal experiences/Performance Investigation Experience

symptom: Problems with Users, Sessions, Events, Funnels, User Flows, or Cohorts panes - missing data, unexpected results, or blade not loading
solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-User-Behavior-Analytics-TSG.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Application Insights portal experiences/User behavior analytics

symptom: Application Insights availability test limit: cannot create more than 100 availability tests per Application Insights resource
rootCause: Fixed service limit of 100 availability tests per Application Insights resource. ARM also limits 800 microsoft.insights/webtest resources per resource group.
solution: Create additional Application Insights resources (each allows 100 more tests). For >800 tests in a subscription, assign different resource groups. These limits are hard and cannot be adjusted.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Availability Tests/Availability tests limits

symptom: Cannot configure availability test to specifically check for HTTP 301 redirect status code; test client automatically follows redirects
rootCause: The availability test client automatically follows HTTP 301 redirects by design. There is no option to disable redirect following in portal-based tests.
solution: Use TrackAvailability() custom test implementation to handle 301 checks manually without automatic redirect following.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Availability Tests/Create or manage availability tests

symptom: Cannot add custom HOST or User-Agent HTTP header to Application Insights availability test; headers are overwritten by test client
rootCause: HOST and User-Agent headers are reserved by the availability test client and automatically overwritten with the test URL hostname and AppInsights user agent string.
solution: Use TrackAvailability() custom test to pass custom HOST or User-Agent header values. Portal-based tests do not support customizing these reserved headers.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Availability Tests/Create or manage availability tests

symptom: Need availability test frequency shorter than 5 minutes; minimum test interval is 5 minutes in portal-based tests
rootCause: The shortest interval for running portal-based availability tests is every 5 minutes. This is a fixed limitation of the service.
solution: Use TrackAvailability() custom function (e.g., Azure Function with timer trigger) for more frequent or custom test intervals.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Availability Tests/Create or manage availability tests

symptom: Error creating availability tests: 'You do not have sufficient permissions to create availability tests. Application Insights Component Contributor access at the resource group level is required'
rootCause: Webtest (microsoft.insights/webtests) resources are separate ARM resources from Application Insights components. Permissions must be granted at subscription or resource group scope, not just the App Insights resource itself.
solution: Assign Application Insights Component Contributor or Monitoring Contributor built-in role at the resource group or subscription scope to grant Microsoft.Insights/webtests/* permissions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Availability Tests/Create or manage availability tests

symptom: Availability test names missing from portal, metrics chart shows no data, or tests appear disabled/greyed out in Application Insights Availability pane
rootCause: Common causes: incorrect time range filter (default 24h), Daily Cap reached preventing telemetry ingestion, Log Analytics workspace deleted or recreated (breaking registration), or insufficient RBAC permissions for webtests resources.
solution: Check time range filter, verify Daily Cap status, ensure Log Analytics workspace exists and is linked to App Insights, verify permissions for Microsoft.Insights/webtests/* at resource group scope. Run availabilityResults query in Logs to confirm data exists.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Availability Tests/Create or manage availability tests

symptom: Unexpected or unidentified HTTP requests arriving at web server from Application Insights availability test agents (User-Agent contains AppInsights)
rootCause: An availability test resource (microsoft.insights/webtests) is configured to target the web application URL, generating synthetic monitoring traffic.
solution: Check User-Agent header for 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; MSIE 9.0; Windows NT 6.1; Trident/5.0; AppInsights)'. Use Azure Resource Graph Explorer query on microsoft.insights/webtests to find matching tests by URL segment. Disable or adjust identified tests.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Availability Tests/Create or manage availability tests

symptom: Availability test telemetry shows 'Other values' for test names, locations, or other dimensions in Application Insights metrics
rootCause: Standard metrics cap dimension cardinality at 100 distinct values. Custom TrackAvailability() with >100 unique test names or locations triggers non-deterministic replacement with 'Other values' during ingestion.
solution: Ensure client applications emit a fixed set of availability test names and locations that does not exceed 100 distinct values per dimension field.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Availability Tests/Monitor internal or on-premises sites (TrackAvailability)

symptom: SSL certificate validation in Standard availability test only checks the final redirected URL certificate, not intermediate redirect endpoints
rootCause: Standard availability test SSL validation only reviews the SSL certificate of the final destination URL after following all HTTP redirects. Intermediate redirect URLs are not validated.
solution: Create separate availability tests targeting non-redirect URLs for each endpoint. Or use TrackAvailability() for custom validation of each SSL certificate in the redirect chain.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Availability Tests/SSL certificate monitoring

symptom: No notification received for expiring or invalid SSL certificate from Standard availability test despite SSL validation being configured
rootCause: SSL validation and proactive lifetime checks require explicit enablement. Alerts use stateful behavior (notify once on first failure). External notification requires separate action group configuration.
solution: Verify SSL validation checkbox is enabled on the Standard test. Confirm proactive lifetime check timeframe setting. Configure alert action groups under Alerts section for external notifications via email or other channels.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Availability Tests/SSL certificate monitoring

symptom: Availability tests fail from US-based regions with TLS-related errors while site is accessible from other regions or browsers
rootCause: Some availability test regions (notably US-based) attempt TLS 1.3 connections. If the target web server does not support TLS 1.3, the connection fails in those regions.
solution: Verify web server supports TLS 1.3 connections. Check .webtestresult file (open with Visual Studio 2019) to confirm TLS version used. Update server TLS configuration to support TLS 1.3.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Availability Tests/Tests show failure, but site is healthy

symptom: Availability test content validation fails; portal shows truncated response body with '<<Truncated>>' but content match string may exist in full response
rootCause: Portal UI and ASC truncate the response body display, but content match logic runs against the full untruncated response payload. The truncation is display-only.
solution: Download the .webtestresult file from End-to-end transaction details view to see the full un-truncated response payload and verify content match string against the complete response.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Availability Tests/Tests show failure, but site is healthy

symptom: Intermittent availability test DNS resolution failures (NameResolutionFailure) despite site being accessible; failures persist longer than expected due to negative DNS caching
rootCause: Transient DNS failures from UDP-based resolution across public internet. Failed DNS lookups are cached per SOA record TTL (often 15 minutes), causing extended failure periods even after transient issue resolves.
solution: Check failure ratio vs total tests (e.g., 10/10080 = 0.1%). Review DNS TTL and SOA record settings. Adjust alert location failure threshold (2 to 5). For persistent issues, engage Azure DNS team with exact timestamps and domain name.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Availability Tests/Tests show failure, but site is healthy

symptom: Availability test fails with duration of exactly 21 or 42 seconds, indicating TCP connection timeout from test agent to target endpoint
rootCause: TCP connection timeout from availability test VMs. Firewall, gateway, or edge network device is blocking the TCP connection attempt from availability test agent IP addresses.
solution: Configure firewall to allow TCP connections from availability test IPs. Use ApplicationInsightsAvailability service tag for Azure NSGs. Download IP ranges from Azure IP Ranges JSON files.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Availability Tests/Tests show failure, but site is healthy

symptom: Availability test returns HTTP 404 on Linux-hosted web application despite correct URL; caused by HTTP verb casing sensitivity
rootCause: Linux web servers are case-sensitive to HTTP verb casing. The availability test may use 'Get' instead of 'GET' as the HTTP verb, which Linux servers reject with 404.
solution: Recreate the web test with proper HTTP verb casing using uppercase 'GET'. Delete and recreate the availability test resource with correct verb casing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Availability Tests/Tests show failure, but site is healthy

symptom: Availability test fails with 301, 302, 401, or 500 on website requiring authentication (AAD/OAuth/SSO/certificate/basic auth); test sends anonymous requests
rootCause: Availability tests send anonymous HTTP requests without authentication tokens or credentials. Sites requiring AAD, OAuth, OIDC, SSO, certificate, or basic auth reject anonymous access.
solution: Add authentication headers manually in test config. Use TrackAvailability() to programmatically supply auth credentials. Or use a dedicated health endpoint allowing anonymous access (Health Endpoint Monitoring pattern).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Availability Tests/Tests show failure, but site is healthy

symptom: Availability test reports success while actual website is down; users cannot access site but test passes due to CDN caching
rootCause: CDN (Azure Front Door, Akamai, Cloudflare, etc.) caches the tested resource. When origin server is down, CDN serves cached content, making availability test pass.
solution: Check if site uses CDN via cdnplanet.com/tools/cdnfinder. Review CDN caching configuration. Test a URL that bypasses cache or test origin endpoint directly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Availability Tests/Tests show success, but site is down

symptom: Availability test shows success despite intermittent application failures; brief outages not detected due to automatic retry feature
rootCause: 'Enable retries for availability test failures' setting causes automatic retry after initial failure. Failure is only reported after 3 consecutive failed attempts, masking brief issues.
solution: Disable the retry feature in availability test settings if immediate failure detection is required. Retry reduces false positives but may mask short-lived outages.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Availability Tests/Tests show success, but site is down

symptom: Enable Application Insights option is greyed out when creating App Service resource (Web Apps, Azure Function) in Azure portal
rootCause: Resource providers enforce workspace-based Application Insights components which require Log Analytics Contributor role access. Without this role, the option is disabled.
solution: Assign Log Analytics Contributor role to the user at the appropriate scope (resource group or subscription) to enable creating workspace-based Application Insights resources.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Access and permissions (RBAC)

symptom: Higher billing charges observed after migrating Application Insights from classic to workspace-based deployment model
rootCause: Migration from classic to workspace-based Application Insights changes the data flow and pricing model. Data now goes through Log Analytics workspace which may have different pricing tier or commitment tier settings.
solution: Review Log Analytics workspace pricing tier and commitment tier settings. Check for duplicate ingestion. See internal known issue #75855 for detailed guidance on billing impact after classic-to-workspace migration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Billing and cost management

symptom: Configuring firewall or network to allow Application Insights availability test traffic through hybrid, on-premises, or Azure NSG firewalls
solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-Firewall-and-network.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Availability Tests/Firewall and network

symptom: Questions about Application Insights Availability Tests SLA Report workbook: missing data, customization, loading errors, or understanding report output
solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-SLA-Report-workbook.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Availability Tests/SLA Report workbook

symptom: Application Insights billing and cost management: explaining usage spikes, understanding SLA credits, calculating service credits, pricing tier questions
solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-Billing-and-cost-management.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Billing and cost management

symptom: Troubleshooting RBAC permissions and access issues for Application Insights: custom roles, built-in roles, access denied errors
solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-Access-and-permissions-RBAC.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Access and permissions (RBAC)

symptom: Data Retention button greyed out in Azure portal for workspace-based Application Insights component, cannot change retention settings
rootCause: Workspace-based AI components manage retention through the underlying Log Analytics workspace, not through the AI portal UI
solution: Configure retention on the Log Analytics workspace directly. Use portal 'Usage and estimated costs' or REST API for per-table retention
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Daily cap, data retention, service limits

symptom: Application Insights data seems missing after reducing retention period then restoring it back to original value
rootCause: When retention period is shortened, Azure waits 30 days before removing data to prevent accidental data loss in error configuration
solution: Wait up to 30 days after reducing retention. Data removal is intentionally delayed. See MS Learn 'How retention and archiving work'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Daily cap, data retention, service limits

symptom: Application Insights data retention period shorter than expected after changing backend Log Analytics workspace
rootCause: Application Insights does not stitch data from a previous LA workspace. Data stitching only applies to migrated components or distributed operations
solution: Data from previous workspace remains accessible only through that workspace. Configure retention on the new workspace separately
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Daily cap, data retention, service limits

symptom: Application Insights Daily Cap exceeded by large amount despite being configured correctly
rootCause: Not using a regional endpoint (connection string). When only instrumentation key is used, Daily Cap tracking is inaccurate causing significant overshoot
solution: Use connection strings with regional endpoints. Check global endpoint usage in ASC. Also check for DCR rules filtering data between AI and LA daily caps
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Daily cap, data retention, service limits

symptom: Cannot configure Daily Cap reset time in Application Insights, setting is not available or not honored
rootCause: Daily Cap reset time is no longer user-configurable. Platform standardized reset times for multi-tenant environment
solution: By design. Daily Cap reset time is managed by the platform. No plans to make it configurable again
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Daily cap, data retention, service limits

symptom: Azure portal only allows setting Application Insights Daily Cap up to 1TB but customer needs higher value up to 3TB
rootCause: Portal UI limits Daily Cap to 1TB. Actual platform maximum is 3TB via PowerShell. Values above 3TB silently ignored
solution: Use PowerShell cmdlet to set values up to 3TB. For >3TB: use separate AI components per application or file ICM to PG. Throttling increase (32k/sec default): up to 10x average usage
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Daily cap, data retention, service limits

symptom: 3rd party Marketplace deployment fails: Region does not support Classic resource mode for Application Insights
rootCause: Third-party marketplace solutions still using Classic AI resource templates no longer supported since Sept 2023
solution: Direct user to contact 3rd party vendor to update ARM templates to workspace-based mode. Microsoft support cannot fix vendor templates
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Deploy or update resources

symptom: Application Insights resource not found or portal shows stale data after ARM deployment or PowerShell/CLI operation
rootCause: ARM cache synchronization delay after control plane operations
solution: Follow ARM cache sync procedure in wiki 'Synchronizing Cache for Caching Issues'. Use Cloud Shell or Try It to isolate auth issues
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Deploy or update resources

symptom: Cannot delete managed Log Analytics workspace or managed resource group created by Application Insights auto-migration
rootCause: Deny Assignment prevents deletion while AI component is linked. Naming pattern: RG=ai_{name}_{id}_managed, WS=managed-{name}-ws
solution: Delete linked AI component (auto-deletes managed WS after ~10 min), or re-associate AI to different workspace then delete managed resource group
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Managed Log Analytics workspaces

symptom: Cannot add tags, custom tables, DCR, or other resources to Application Insights managed workspace resource group
rootCause: Deny Assignment blocks: new workspace creation, tag operations, custom tables, DCR additions, shared key access. Only allows daily cap quota changes
solution: By design. Re-associate AI to user-owned LA workspace for full control. Do not rely on managed workspaces for production
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Managed Log Analytics workspaces

symptom: Application Insights data collected before resource move to new subscription/resource group is inaccessible in portal
rootCause: Moving workspace-based AI changes Resource ID. AI portal uses resource ID to query data, making pre-move data inaccessible through portal
solution: Query pre-move data from Log Analytics Logs blade using _ResourceId column. Known limitation in MS Learn move-support-resources
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Move, rename, delete resources

symptom: All telemetry stops flowing to Application Insights after configuring AMPLS and disabling public access
rootCause: Client-side JavaScript SDK telemetry from users not on private network cannot reach private endpoint. Free-tier App Service has no VNet support
solution: Ensure all sources on private network, or set AMPLS to Open mode. For JS SDK: use separate AI component or APIM proxy
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Network isolation and Private Link (AMPLS)

symptom: Private endpoint for Application Insights AMPLS in failed provisioning state causing dropped data
rootCause: Exhausted subnet IPs or concurrent AMPLS resource additions causing race condition
solution: Check subnet IP availability, remove and recreate private endpoint. See Known Issue #64682
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Network isolation and Private Link (AMPLS)

symptom: AMPLS DNS resolves to wrong private IP or no IP address, Application Insights telemetry not flowing
rootCause: Second AMPLS on different network sharing same DNS server overwrote entries, or custom DNS not forwarding to Azure DNS 168.63.129.16
solution: Wrong IP: remove and recreate private endpoint to refresh DNS. No IP: verify DNS server connectivity and Azure DNS forwarding. Check NSGs between VMs and endpoints
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Network isolation and Private Link (AMPLS)

symptom: Need to purge specific telemetry data from Application Insights but no UI option available in portal
rootCause: No built-in UI for data purge. Must use Workspace Purge REST API with KQL query
solution: Use Workspace Purge REST API. Test KQL in Logs blade first. Save operation ID for status tracking via Get Purge Status API. May take days
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Purge telemetry

symptom: Need to recover accidentally deleted Application Insights component and restore telemetry data
rootCause: AI supports soft-delete recovery. For workspace-based, LA workspace must be recovered first if also deleted
solution: 1. Recover LA workspace via soft-delete. 2. Use ASC to find recoverable AI components. 3. Follow recovery procedure. Use Azure Lock to prevent future deletion
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Recover deleted resource

symptom: Application Insights telemetry dropped with reason code U or V after disabling local auth for Entra ID authentication
rootCause: Entra ID auth not correctly configured. Could be unsupported SDK version or incorrect credential setup
solution: Check SDK minimum version per language. Use Managed Identity for production. Check Dropped by Reason U/V/F in ASC. See MS Learn troubleshooting
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Secure ingestion with Entra ID

symptom: JavaScript SDK cannot use Entra ID authentication for Application Insights, no auth configuration option exists
rootCause: JavaScript SDK does not support Entra ID authentication. Current platform limitation with no ETA
solution: Use separate AI component with local auth for JS telemetry, or use Azure APIM as proxy with Entra auth. Do not disable local auth if JS SDK is required
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deploy or manage Application Insights resources/Secure ingestion with Entra ID

symptom: Application Insights data retention settings not preserved after Classic to workspace-based migration, drops to 90 days
rootCause: Migration does not carry over Classic retention settings. LA workspace defaults to 90 days for AI data types
solution: Manually configure LA workspace retention after migration. Use REST API for per-table or portal for workspace-wide retention
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deprecated features/Classic Application Insights retirement

symptom: Continuous Export stops working after migrating Application Insights from Classic to workspace-based
rootCause: Continuous Export deprecated, not supported for workspace-based. Must disable before migration
solution: Replace with Diagnostic Settings (different schema, breaks Stream Analytics). Consider LA archive policy for cost-effective long-term storage
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deprecated features/Classic Application Insights retirement

symptom: Classic Application Insights stopped ingesting telemetry after June 2025, no new data collected
rootCause: Microsoft disabled ingestion for Classic AI on June 19, 2025. Auto-migration with managed workspaces completed globally by early June 2025
solution: Convert to workspace-based Application Insights. Existing data accessible per retention (90 days default). AMPLS resources migrated June 16-18
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deprecated features/Classic Application Insights retirement

symptom: Classic URL ping test retirement notice - need to migrate availability tests to standard tests before Sept 2026
rootCause: Classic URL ping tests retiring September 30, 2026. No automated migration confirmed yet
solution: Follow MS Learn availability-test-migration. Check pricing changes. Use PowerShell script to identify AI resources with web tests and check Kind property
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deprecated features/Classic availability test retirement

symptom: Internal (Microsoft) users see Failed to create API Key error in Azure Portal when trying to create a new Application Insights API key
rootCause: As part of SFI (Secure Future Initiative), Microsoft has blocked creation of new API keys by internal customers ahead of the public retirement. External customers can still create API keys. Existing keys continue to work until full retirement.
solution: API Keys are deprecated. Migrate to Microsoft Entra ID authentication for querying Application Insights data. For custom solutions (Function App, Web App) see Entra ID authentication guides. For Logic Apps use the Azure Monitor Logs connector.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deprecated features/Data Access API key retirement

symptom: Application Insights API key returns error when calling REST API on a workspace-based resource
rootCause: Backend Log Analytics workspace Access Control Mode is set to Require workspace permissions, which requires Azure AD auth tokens. API key (SAS key) authentication is blocked in this mode.
solution: Open the Properties pane of the backend LA workspace and change Access Control Mode to Use resource or workspace permissions. Alternatively migrate to Entra ID authentication instead of API keys.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deprecated features/Data Access API key retirement

symptom: Live Metrics (Quick Pulse) stops working after September 30 2025 for Application Insights resources still using API key authentication
rootCause: Microsoft is retiring API key authentication for Live Metrics on September 30 2025. After this date Live Metrics will not function unless the application has switched to Entra ID authentication.
solution: Switch from API key to Microsoft Entra ID authentication for Live Metrics before the retirement date. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/azure-ad-authentication and https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/live-stream#secure-the-control-channel
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deprecated features/Live Metrics API key retirement

symptom: Duplicate telemetry data appears after enabling Diagnostic Settings on a workspace-based Application Insights resource
rootCause: Diagnostic Settings for workspace-based Application Insights collects the same data that Application Insights itself already sends to the Log Analytics workspace. When the destination is the same workspace, all data is duplicated.
solution: Create the Diagnostic Setting to send data to a DIFFERENT Log Analytics workspace or another destination (Storage Account, Event Hub). Do not point Diagnostic Settings to the same workspace the Application Insights resource is based on.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Diagnostic settings/Duplicate data after enabling

symptom: Exported data schema changes when migrating from Continuous Export to Diagnostic Settings for Application Insights
rootCause: Diagnostic Settings uses a fundamentally different schema than Continuous Export by design. There is no workaround or configuration to preserve the old Continuous Export schema format.
solution: This is expected behavior. Adjust downstream data consumers and pipelines to handle the new Diagnostic Settings schema. No alternative schema format is available. Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/export-telemetry#diagnostic-settings-based-export
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Diagnostic settings/Exported Data Schema Changed

symptom: Recently created telemetry records missing from expected Storage Account folder in Diagnostic Settings export, or records appear in unexpected folder paths
rootCause: Behavior change: Diagnostic Settings Export to Storage now writes telemetry based on received/ingestion time instead of log creation time (_timestamp_). Delayed log entries go to the ingestion-time folder, not the creation-time folder.
solution: Look for missing records in Storage folders within 48 hours of when the record was originally created. The newest folder always contains the most recently processed logs. Adjust downstream processing to account for ingestion-time-based folder paths.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Diagnostic settings/No data at export destination

symptom: No historical/pre-existing data appears at Diagnostic Settings export destination after enabling. Only newly ingested data is exported.
rootCause: Diagnostic Settings (like Continuous Export) does not support backfilling data ingested before enablement. Only data arriving after the setting is enabled will be exported.
solution: For backfilling critical data: (1) Use Log Analytics API to programmatically query and write data (has row/size limits). (2) Use Logic Apps with Export data from Log Analytics to Storage approach. (3) For large/high-profile customers, escalate to SRE team with TA/SME approval for one-time export (requires special approval).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Diagnostic settings/Recover or backfill exported data

symptom: Python Application Insights SDK based on OpenCensus is deprecated/retired (since Sept 30 2024). Telemetry still ingested but no support or fixes available.
rootCause: OpenCensus Python SDK was officially retired on September 30, 2024. OpenCensus was a predecessor of OpenTelemetry and its GitHub repositories are now closed. No further patches or support will be provided.
solution: Migrate to Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Python Distro. Follow official migration guide: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/opentelemetry-python-opencensus-migrate. Be aware of documented Changes and limitations during migration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deprecated features/Legacy SDK support

solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-data-access-api-key-retirement.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deprecated features/Data Access API key retirement

symptom: Instrumentation key retirement and migration to connection strings - global vs regional endpoints, TLS requirements, APIM/Power Platform/B2C RP migration specifics
solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-instrumentation-keys-retirement.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Deprecated features/Instrumentation keys retirement

symptom: No data at Diagnostic Settings export destination (Storage, Event Hub, or Log Analytics) - multi-step troubleshooting covering DS configuration validation, data pipeline verification, ingestion-time behavior change
solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-no-data-at-export-destination.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Diagnostic settings/No data at export destination

symptom: Recover or backfill missing exported data from Diagnostic Settings - programmatic recovery via API, Logic Apps approach, SRE escalation for large customers
solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-recover-backfill-exported-data.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Diagnostic settings/Recover or backfill exported data

symptom: Logic Apps integration with Application Insights stops working; deprecated Application Insights connector fails after API key retirement
rootCause: Legacy Application Insights connector for Logic Apps relied on API Keys which have been retired. The old connector is deprecated and no longer functional.
solution: Use Azure Monitor Logs connector (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/connectors/azuremonitorlogs/) instead of the deprecated Application Insights connector.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Integration with Application Insights/Logic Apps

symptom: Power BI shows fewer rows than expected from Application Insights query; e.g. query returns 750K rows but only 500K appear in Power BI
rootCause: REST API has dual hard limits: maximum number of rows AND maximum total data size. Both limits apply independently.
solution: Query data in batches to work around API limits. See Azure Monitor Service Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/service-limits#la-query-api
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Integration with Application Insights/Power BI

symptom: API Key (SAS Key) authentication fails for workspace-based Application Insights REST API queries
rootCause: Backend Log Analytics workspace Access control mode set to Require workspace permissions blocks SAS Key/API Key authentication.
solution: Open Properties pane of backend LA Workspace. Change Access control mode to Use resource or workspace permissions. Generate and test with a new API key.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Integration with Application Insights/REST API access

symptom: REST API returns 499 response codes for Application Insights or Log Analytics draft API queries
rootCause: Client times out the request before the draft API returns a response. Caused by concurrent API calls from dashboards or Grafana (default 30s timeout).
solution: Extend client-side query timeout. For Grafana, increase default 30-second timeout. Reduce concurrent draft API calls.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Integration with Application Insights/REST API access

symptom: Application Insights client_IP field shows 0.0.0.0 or blank; no IP address collected in telemetry
rootCause: IP collection/unmasking is not enabled by default. Application Insights masks IP addresses to 0.0.0.0 unless explicitly configured.
solution: Enable IP address collection: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/ip-collection?tabs=net#storage-of-ip-address-data
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Missing or incorrect telemetry and performance issues/Client IP and geolocation

symptom: Application Insights SDK logs internal 503 ServiceUnavailable error codes in trace telemetry
rootCause: SDK temporarily unable to communicate with Application Insights ingestion service (transient connectivity issue).
solution: Informational only - no action required. SDK automatically retries per telemetry channel retry logic. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/telemetry-channels
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Missing or incorrect telemetry and performance issues/Crashes or slowness after enabling

solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-Azure-Dashboards-AI-Integration-TSG.md

solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-AI-Workbooks-TSG.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Integration with Application Insights/Built-in workbooks

solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-Crashes-Slowness-After-Enabling-AI.md

symptom: Application Insights telemetry appears delayed; IngestionTime field shows large gap after customer recently changed retention settings on the table
rootCause: Changing retention settings on a Log Analytics table causes the IngestionTime field to be reset to the time of the retention change, resulting in falsely reported ingestion delays (Known Issue #61748)
solution: Verify if retention settings were recently changed. If so, the delay is cosmetic -- IngestionTime was reset at the time of retention change and will normalize for newly ingested data. No action needed beyond customer education.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Missing or incorrect telemetry and performance issues/Delayed telemetry

symptom: Application Insights shows exact duplicate telemetry records at high rates; all or most records appear twice in Log Analytics queries
rootCause: Diagnostic Settings enabled on the Application Insights resource configured to send allLogs to the backing Log Analytics workspace, causing telemetry to be written twice -- once via normal SDK ingestion and once via Diagnostic Settings export
solution: Run Telemetry Flow Diagnostic Script (Test-AppInsightsTelemetryFlow.ps1) to confirm. Remove or modify the Diagnostic Settings on the Application Insights resource to stop exporting allLogs to the same LA workspace. Add case number to Known Issue #22050.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Missing or incorrect telemetry and performance issues/Duplicate telemetry

symptom: Python application sends duplicate telemetry to Application Insights
rootCause: Multiple Application Insights SDKs/distros enabled simultaneously -- either OpenCensus SDK alongside OpenTelemetry Distro, or OpenTelemetry Exporter alongside OpenTelemetry Distro
solution: Ensure only one SDK/Distro is active at any time. Disable the deprecated OpenCensus SDK if using OpenTelemetry Distro. Remove the standalone OpenTelemetry Exporter if using the Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Distro.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Missing or incorrect telemetry and performance issues/Duplicate telemetry

symptom: Custom metrics stop reporting or are dropped in Application Insights; metrics work initially then stop after some time
rootCause: Exceeded one of four custom metric limits: 50,000 active time series, 64KB combined UTF-8 metric names, 10 dimension keys per metric, or 256 character length for namespaces/names/dimension keys/values
solution: Review metric cardinality and reduce unique dimension value combinations to stay under 50K active time series. Check Known Issue #27792. Reduce dimension count to <= 10 per metric.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Missing or incorrect telemetry and performance issues/Incorrect telemetry values (fields, metrics, counters)

symptom: cloud_RoleName and cloud_RoleInstance fields are empty or missing in Application Insights telemetry for applications running outside standard Azure PaaS services
rootCause: Application Insights SDKs auto-populate cloud_RoleName only for known Azure resource types (App Services, Functions). Custom or external/on-premises environments do not provide this metadata automatically.
solution: For OpenTelemetry SDKs, set OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES env var: service.name=custom-role-name,service.instance.id=custom-role-instance. For classic SDKs, implement a custom TelemetryInitializer to set cloud role name.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Missing or incorrect telemetry and performance issues/Incorrect telemetry values (fields, metrics, counters)

symptom: Custom Track calls (TrackEvent, TrackTrace, TrackMetric) lost or not appearing in Application Insights after auto-instrumentation enabled on App Service
rootCause: Auto-instrumentation with XDT_MicrosoftApplicationInsights_PreemptSdk=true preempts the manual SDK, silently dropping custom telemetry calls made via the classic API
solution: Check App Settings for XDT_MicrosoftApplicationInsights_PreemptSdk. If manual SDK with custom Track calls is needed, disable auto-instrumentation or set PreemptSdk to false.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Missing or incorrect telemetry and performance issues/Missing telemetry types

symptom: Hundreds or thousands of telemetry items share the same operation ID in Application Insights distributed tracing
rootCause: Client application (especially SPAs) sends multiple HTTP requests to backend with the same traceparent/Request-Id correlation header, causing all requests to inherit one shared operation ID
solution: Identify the calling client. For SPAs, enable enableAutoRouteTracking in JavaScript SDK config. For other clients, ensure each HTTP request uses a distinct traceparent header value.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Missing or incorrect telemetry and performance issues/Distributed tracing or correlation issues

symptom: Intermittent or partial telemetry loss; some telemetry fields appear stripped, modified, or record counts do not match expected
rootCause: Data Collection Rules (DCRs) with Workspace Transforms configured on the Log Analytics workspace reshape, filter, or drop fields/records before data reaches the destination tables
solution: Run Telemetry Flow Diagnostic Script to detect active DCRs. Review DCR transformation KQL queries for unintended filtering. See wiki: Check for Data Collection Rules (DCRs) and Ingestion Time Transformations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Topics/Missing or incorrect telemetry and performance issues/Intermittent telemetry

symptom: Delayed telemetry diagnosis: client-side vs ingestion-side delay with network, proxy, DNS, and outage checks
solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-delayed-telemetry.md

solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-distributed-tracing.md
